programme.desc: While on medical leave, Ducky reopens a 12-year-old case by talking officials at a cemetery into releasing the body of a navy commander whose post-mortem he carried out 12 years previously. Crime drama, starring David McCallum

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Director Vance charges DiNozzo with a special assignment to go to France, locate Admiral Kendall's wayward daughter Amanda and bring her home. After eventually tracking down Amanda, the pair embark on a rollercoaster ride to the relative safety of the nearest NCIS office in Spain. Michael Weatherly stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs throws himself back into his work following his near-death experience, updating his appearance and tackling a new risky case alongside DEA agent Luis Mitchell, who wants help bringing down a drugs cartel. Concerned about his colleague's apparent enthusiasm to re-enter the field, DiNozzo discovers Mitchell is the son of a former NCIS agent who gave his life trying to save Gibbs' late wife and daughter, and this attachment is causing Gibbs to break his own rule against personal involvement in a case. However, a significant danger is posed by Benson Long, the cartel's head, and Mitchell's top target

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Sleeplessness and a taxing workload catch up with Jack, who is lucky to escape from a road accident so bizarre his colleagues begin to suspect he is losing the plot. programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Connie performs two vital operations as Faye's son Archie is brought in fighting for his life after swallowing a whistle and Maddy's suspected killer is admitted with stomach pain. Michael outlines his vision for the future of the hospital, announcing the arrival of a matron to oversee the surgical wards, but Mark is vocal in his opposition to the newcomer. Shelagh McLeod joins the cast

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Comfort is hit by two cruel blows as she tracks down a woman who claims to have been married to Fin, and receives shocking news from the police. programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Leela and Tony discover the body of a supermodel who had recently fronted an anti-fur campaign despite her lack of real conviction. Lorna notes that the crime scene has been arranged to resemble a shot from the film, suggesting animal rights activists may have resented her hypocrisy. Subsequent investigations reveal a host of rival male admirers with reason to kill her and Terry digs up evidence of a more sinister side to the woman's life

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil turns to Pat in desperation. Pauline feels guilty about a telephone call and Blossom takes Tiffany to task

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Frank has another breakdown as Phil watches every move he makes. Alan receives bad news and Pat's visitor proves too much for her

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: Major Tom Cadman discovers that his wife has been having an affair with a rival officer whilst he has been in Norther Ireland

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: Henry is enlisted in the search for a missing policeman, but the restaurant's opening night is looming. Detective drama, starring Richard Griffiths and Maggie Steed

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Siegfried gives another lecture on economy to Calum, who is visited by Molly McFeely, with whose family he stayed while in Ireland - while Deirdre also pays a call at the same time. Meanwhile, James receives an invitation to examine Lord Buttermere's new stallion. Rural drama, starring John McGlynn, Robert Hardy and Christopher Timothy

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: Audrey moves out of Grantleigh Manor, and Richard and his mother move in, disappointing the former resident with their new ways. Comedy, starring Penelope Keith and Peter Bowles

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: A romantic invitation from Mr Lucas to Miss Brahms is delivered to Mrs Slocombe by mistake. Shop-floor comedy, with Trevor Bannister, Mollie Sugden and Wendy Richard. First broadcast in 1973

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: A man foolishly believes a curse has been placed on him, so Truly, Entwistle and Alvin feel it is their duty to perform an exorcism. Peter Sallis and Frank Thornton star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Barry becomes dejected when his fitness-mad neighbour Boothroyd begins to outshine him in all things physical. However, he discovers that when it comes to karate, he may just have an advantage over his new rival. Long-running comedy, guest starring Brian Conley

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Radio quiz Up to You comes to Kembleford and the sleuth suspects one of the cast is responsible when the landlady of the house they are staying at is murdered. Mark Williams stars

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: It seems somebody has taken the island's Day of the Dead celebrations a little too literally when a man receives a voicemail from his wife saying she is being attacked. When she turns up dead, suspicion falls upon her friends and acquaintances at the nearby yacht club - her husband Finn, club secretary Charlotte and chairman Hugh. However, Jack's curiosity is piqued by a yellow butterfly found on the victim's clothing and he calls upon Commissioner Selwyn's knowledge of such matters. Caribbean whodunit, guest starring John Sessions and Pippa Haywood

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Ricky Hanson faces trial for attempted murder, and his lawyer sets about trying to discredit each member of the squad. However, Jack receives a welcome distraction when an old friend asks him to reinvestigate the death of a wealthy businessman, and he, Brian and Gerry spring into action. Guest starring David Troughton, with Amanda Redman, James Bolam, Alun Armstrong and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: When a skull is found buried in concrete in a church crypt, Boyd seeks to uncover who it belongs to and why it is there, but gets little help from the Church authorities, who seem focused on obscuring the facts. The discovery of another body, adorned with ritualistic symbols, leads the team to a suspicious workman and an uncooperative retired priest. Trevor Eve and Sue Johnston star in a feature-length episode, with guest appearances by Annette Crosbie and Barry Morse

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Cromwell manoeuvres a bill through Parliament acknowledging Henry as head of the Church of England, the first step in enabling the king to grant his own divorce from Katherine of Aragon. The Boleyn family also looks to the lawyer to deal with Harry Percy, whose wife claims her own marriage is unlawful on the grounds that her husband had previously made a binding contract of marriage with Anne Boleyn. This is a task Cromwell approaches with relish, remembering how Percy helped bring down Wolsey. Historical drama, starring Mark Rylance, Damian Lewis and Claire Foy

Leela reports an attack by an intruder but she is put in a compromising situation when her colleagues turn up and discover a bag of heroin in her flat

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Roy is faced with an uninvited guest at his party, while Pat falls apart. Meanwhile, Nigel gets a surprising offer

programme.desc: Calum cooks a meal, but it takes so long to prepare his guests drink too much and pass out before it is ready. Meanwhile, James encounters a dog that faints to gain attention. Rural drama, starring Robert Hardy, Christopher Timothy and John McGlynn

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: Audrey reprimands DeVere for not fulfilling his duties as lord of the manor when he fails to attend church on Sunday, but then forgets to practise what she preaches. Penelope Keith and Peter Bowles star

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: The department store staff complain when they are expected to attend a sales conference outside working hours. Comedy, starring Mollie Sugden and Trevor Bannister. First shown in 1973

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Howard becomes ill at ease with his lot in life, and tries to change his appearance so that not even Pearl would recognise him. But he soon discovers that the drawbacks of assuming someone else's identity are greater than the benefits

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Local PCs Cooper and Walsh try to master a new crime-fighting technique. However, the duo's carefully laid plans unravel when Barry decides to treat a retired vicar to a day trip. Long-running sitcom, starring Ken Kitson and Louis Emerick

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates the murder of a Colonial Postal Service officer when the fair comes to Kembleford. Drama, starring Mark Williams and Sorcha Cusack

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The leader of a spiritual retreat is found strangled en route to the contemplation garden, giving Jack plenty to think about as he tries to figure out which member of the group was responsible. The problem is, they were all meditating at the time of the murder, and are able to provide one another with alibis, so how could any of them be the killer? To add to the DI's problems, he is struggling to come up with a speech to honour Commissioner Patterson at an award ceremony. Caribbean whodunit, starring Ardal O'Hanlon

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The inclusion of a letter in a late actor's biography reopens an old case for the team - but reliving the past threatens to be too much for the widow who was accidentally responsible for his death. DAC Strickland's cocky nephew lends his assistance, but his affections for Sandra bring out the worst in Gerry and Brian. Dexter Fletcher guest stars

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. DS Boyd investigates whether a woman who confessed to the killing of her husband 25 years ago could be innocent. Startling new facts come to light when Annie Keel's son, now living under a new identity, has to relive the trauma of the night of the murder. Drama, guest starring Lynda Bellingham, with Trevor Eve and Sue Johnston

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Henry does little to hide his disappointment when his queen gives birth to a girl, with Anne aware that her power in court rests on producing a male heir. With the Tudor line remaining vulnerable, Cromwell decrees that everyone in public life must take an oath to recognise Henry's supremacy as head of the English Church, as well as the legality of his marriage to Anne Boleyn, but Thomas More refuses to swear allegiance and is committed to the Tower. Mark Rylance, Claire Foy, Damian Lewis and Anton Lesser star

However, the scoundrel's skills are put to the test when Marina and Miss Davenport seek his aid in helping Auntie Wainwright crack a keyless safe. John Challis guest stars

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: When Alvin and Entwistle witness Lance attempting to prove to his wife that Elvis Presley is still alive, the duo feel duty-bound to help him out, and organise for the King himself to make an appearance. Long-running comedy starring Brian Murphy, Burt Kwouk and Kenneth Cope

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A missing woman staggers into the police station, claiming a creature is 'trying to get out'. That night her father panics as he sees an alien shape through the window. The next day his body is found in a locked room and Father Brown suspects he has been poisoned. Such events prompt local speculation to mount that Kembleford is under threat of an alien invasion. Mark Williams stars

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team investigates an arson attack on a 1980s music station that resulted in the death of a controversial DJ, and looks into the subsequent suicide of the station's owner. Strickland seeks a permanent replacement for the absent Halford, but Pullman and Standing call his bluff

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Another mystery for Ardal O'Hanlon as Irish detective Jack Mooney working on the Caribbean island of Saint Marie, where the sun shines all day and it seems half the residents are killers-in-waiting. And so it appears this week as someone from JP's past is caught red-handed at a murder scene, raking up old memories for the young officer. But can the DI unravel the complex mystery to get at the truth? Tobi Bakare and Danny John-Jules co-star, with guest appearances from Ralph Brown and Adjoa Andoh

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The body of police hero Debbie Britten washes up in the Thames a year after she disappeared, summoning the Cold Case unit to investigate. Initially, suspicion falls on a stalker, but the plot thickens further with the discovery that Debbie was pregnant at the time of her murder. Trevor Eve, Sue Johnston, Holly Aird and Tessa Peake-Jones star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Terry goes undercover, hoping to arrest a supplier of illegally imported goods, but gets caught up in a pub brawl between rival football fans in which a fellow officer is stabbed. Lewis's inside knowledge is called upon when another spate of gang violence breaks out and the new leader of the Skens appears to be settling old scores. Guest starring Adrian Lukis

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: The Act of Supremacy has declared Henry the supreme head of the church in England, but Holy Roman emperor Charles V and his ambassador Eustache Chapuys refuse to recognise the new title or the monarch's divorce and subsequent marriage. With Anne pregnant and away from court, Henry begins to take more notice of Jane Seymour, whose family enlist Cromwell's help in their dealings with the king - much to Anne's displeasure. Historical drama, starring Mark Rylance, Damian Lewis, Clare Foy and Mathieu Amalric

programme.desc: Stories of holiday-makers left stranded at the airport because their Covid-19 test results came back too late, and people who arrived at a hotel only to discover it was closed

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit two neighbouring cottages in Derbyshire, a terrace in Bolton and a semi in Leeds before they are sold under the hammer

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Claire Duffy wanted the best in life for her young family, especially youngest daughter Abbie, who had recently received a cystic fibrosis diagnosis. She was sure that the warm weather and outdoor lifestyle of New Zealand would do wonders for Abbie's health, but had to convince home bird husband Russell that moving halfway across the world was a viable option. Two years later, have they made the leap, or was the pull of home just too strong? Nicki Chapman catches up with the family to find out

programme.title: Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A pair of pyjamas reveal the shocking true identity of an attacker who left his victim for dead, and a bright yellow baseball cap puts a daylight robber behind bars

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Caroline Hawley and Roo Irvine help two teams look for items in an antiques centre in the Lancashire town of Eccleston. Eric Knowles overseeing proceedings and also attempts a spot of Morris Dancing, discovering the history of the humble clog and the part it played in Lancashire's industrial heritage

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A new musical director for the bell-ringers of St Mary's leads to discord, disharmony and the blood-curdling sound of murder. Mystery, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers cook comfort food dishes straight from the oven, including a new take on steak and chips and sticky gingerbread

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a house hunter from Surrey to find a new home in Pembrokeshire, and finds her a remote period house with spectacular views within her budget of £900,000

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Hexham's fortnightly market brings hope of selling some lamb and beef to farmer Andrew and his daughter Emma, and Lee and Shelly are selling produce from their Tamworth pigs

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents the programme in which five dealers bid against one another to buy a pedal car, an Edwardian card table, a quirky voice box and a mystery pot

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Jack sets his plan in motion and manages to negotiate an advantageous deal, meaning he soon has good news for Denise. Stacey is in for a surprise, Whitney has a choice to make and Ruby has to come up with an explanation for Martin. Ash has advice for Sonia after she ends another exhausting shift at the hospital

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Sacha buries his head in the sand when Beka is taken ill on New Year's Eve. Kian is on edge as Sarah Jane avoids his calls, and his stash is running out. He tries to convince Jac he is fine when she worries that their relationship is the problem. It is total chaos on AAU, and the F1s are daunted when Hanssen leaves them alone to face their first New Year's night shift

programme.title: The Crash Detectives
programme.desc: Behind the scenes with Gwent Police's dedicated forensic collision investigators as they deal with serious incidents on the roads. In this edition, a hit-and-run leaves a cyclist fighting for her life, while mystery surrounds the death of a delivery driver on a busy dual carriageway

programme.title: Traces
programme.desc: As Kathy and Sarah work on their reports for the Secrets nightclub fire, the emerging picture is that two of the deaths were accidental, but the third victim was dead before the fire started. Sarah's report has some damning things to say about the safety of the building, but soon feeling overwhelmed by the police interest she wonders what she's started. Emma is cautioned by DI McKinven to stop sleuthing, as the stars align to put Daniel Macafee in her path - for whom she feels a deep connection. It later transpires that Daniel harbours a guilty conscience as he was employed by the company that refurbished Secrets. Crime drama by Amelia Bullmore, starring Molly Windsor, Laura Fraser and Jennifer Spence

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: After discovering the network's plan and learning they will not be going to America, David and Michael are dismayed to learn who will be going in their place. The guys are also upset to find out how well Simon is doing without them

programme.title: Accused: A Mother on Trial
programme.desc: Rex searches for evidence to implicate Kennard in the crime. Details of Ashley and Kennard's past reveal why she might be covering up for him. Kennard finally breaks his silence

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: Contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing a cash prize. Helping them this time round are Stacey Dooley, Roman Kemp, Omid Djalili, Judy Murray, Owain Wyn Evans, Kate Humble and Vikkstar

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Dan Walker and Louise Minchin

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville ask who is responsible when valuables are stolen from a coach. Plus, the bride and groom left waiting at the airport

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell view properties in Clapham, London, Maidstone, Kent and Whitehaven, Cumbria

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Lee Price was a huge fan of the Down Under lifestyle, having fallen in love with the place after getting married there. While he thought a move to New Zealand would be a great idea, he needed to convince wife Amanda and son Aaron, who didn't have the same enthusiasm. Could a trial week give them a glimpse into what they might be able to achieve? Three years on, where are the Prices based now?

programme.title: Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a discarded scrap of paper found in a household bin helped convict a killer, and a distinctive mole holds the key to tracking down a dangerous and predatory attacker

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning and Christina Trevanion present from the Shropshire town of Oswestry, where two teams shop for antiques with the help of experts Kate Bliss and Ochuko Ojiri. Christina also visits one of the most historically important buildings in Britain, a flax mill in Shrewsbury that can lay claim to being the world's first skyscraper

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Poor weather plagues Bunty and Mrs McCarthy's picnic. Meanwhile, Father Brown must intervene when it appears that a dark spirit has murderous intent

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers give a traditional pudding a new look, reveal the secrets behind the ultimate fish cake and offer their take on Teesside dish chicken parmo

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: With a top budget of £560,000, Jules Hudson assists a retired couple in their bid to move to Cornwall, where they hope to get involved in the local community

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two sets of farmers - Jack and Nina and Andy and Tim - hope to make a splash with their rapeseed oils and sauces at a market in the small town of Axbridge, Somerset

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers hosts as five dealers bid against each other for items brought in by the public, including odd medical models, a knife sharpener, a drinks cabinet and a toy milk cart

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants have to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present the magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents a programme in which five dealers bid against one another to buy intriguing items brought in by members of the public. The opening edition features an unusual item of hairdressing memorabilia, something salvaged from a tip, a classic piece of industrial furniture and a beloved toy

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team tackle a concertina that survived combat in the First World War, a vintage jigsaw, and a cocktail table with a surprising secret compartment. Plus, soft-toy restoration duo Julie Tatchell and Amanda Middleditch are called upon to rescue a much-loved toy dog, made by German manufacturer Steiff, which its owner Anita has treasured ever since it was given to her by her grandparents as a young girl

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: In the wake of the coronavirus pandemic, the health market has exploded with products promising to boost, support or supercharge our natural defences and protect you from infectious bacteria and viruses. This programme offers a guide to strengthening the immune system during cold and flu season, and examines some of the so-called 'miracle' products, superfoods and supplements that claim to help

programme.title: I Am Greta
programme.desc: The story of teenage climate activist Greta Thunberg, told through never-before-seen footage of the girl who has become the voice of a generation. Starting with her one-person school strike for climate justice outside the Swedish Parliament, the film follows Greta - a shy student with Asperger's - as she rises to prominence, and her galvanising global impact as she sparks school strikes around the world

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: The holiday-makers left footing the bill after insurers refused to pay for their medical emergencies, and the bizarre cases of travellers sent to stay in hotels that did not exist

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The redevelopment of a million-pound-plus house in Fulham, as well as the progress of auction buys in St Helens near Liverpool and Stoke in Staffordshire

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Rebecca Ellis had always dreamt of a move to Australia, inspired by her grandfather's tales of a beautiful and friendly country. Partner Kylie and her four children wanted to support her, but fears of leaving loved ones and the kids' beloved father behind were foremost in their minds. Could a week in Brisbane help them come round to Rebecca's way of thinking? Three years since we last caught up with them, where do they call home - Australia or the UK?

programme.title: Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a single glove uncovered the identities of a prolific and violent gang, and an image of a distinctive ponytail that solved a high-tech bank heist

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Kate Bliss help the teams in Stamford, Lincolnshire, and Natasha Raskin Sharp visits Burghley House to look at The Hell Staircase, a fine Baroque mural

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The sleuthing clergyman uncovers secrets, hypocrisy and murder at a finishing school for young ladies. Mystery, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers cook comfort food oozing with traditional values, including a twist on a classic pudding and a perfect teatime treat

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin is on a house-hunting mission with a couple who have £750,000 to break away from their London suburb and find a rural family home in North Yorkshire

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Proud farmers Jim and Stuart compete in one of the UK's oldest and most prestigious sheepdog trials at Longshaw - but the competition is strong in this part of the Peak District. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers are offered to chance to bid on a vintage stereoscope, two gigantic apothecary glasses and a children's game thought to be from the 19th century

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Patrick's good intentions catch up with him, leaving Denise furious and him heartbroken. Ruby makes a snap admission in an attempt to keep Martin close, while Martin is inspired by Stacey's strength. Gray tells Karen that he wants his kids back for good

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: Dog fanatic Sheridan Smith and her fluffy co-host Stanley are on a mission to find the nation's best professional dog groomer. Heat one sees the first four contestants working on a long-haired shih-tzu - one of the most popular breeds to grace British salons. After that they let their imaginations fly as they give four curly haired canines a teddy bear trim to parade on the dogwalk in front of their owners

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The Caribbean-set whodunit returns for its 10th anniversary series, which promises more murders and the return of one or two familiar faces. A TV news presenter is found dead in her pool at home. DI Neville Parker is convinced her co-host is responsible. But given he was live on air at the time, how could he have done it? The return of an experienced sergeant to the island could be just what Neville needs to crack the case

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: After leaving her native South Africa when her young daughter became critically ill, Zelease Ballentine Brain felt her young children needed to live in a place where being outside in the sunshine was the norm. Husband Matt was naturally cautious, and needed to see that Australia would give the family all that Zelease had talked about. Could a trial week swing him round to her way of thinking and, three years on, where have they decided to settle? Presented by Nicki Chapman

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: The return of the programme celebrating inspirational farming families and the rural events where they showcase their hard work. Sheep and arable farmers John and Ed head to Moseley Farmers' Market, but when they arrive their pitch location threatens to put a dent in their earnings. The Styan family, meanwhile, need to sell as much veg as possible for cash to make some much-needed repairs to their windblown farm

programme.title: Nadiya's American Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain continues her culinary journey with a visit to California, home to one of the most diverse populations in America. She is invited to a Mexican quinceanera - an extravagant fiesta celebrating a girl's 15th birthday - cooks for an all-woman daredevil rodeo riding team and makes handmade fortune cookies with a grandma in one of the last remaining workshops in San Francisco's Chinatown

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Rob Beckett hosts the quiz in which contestants must choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves

programme.title: Coast and Country Auctions
programme.desc: The stories behind Britain's traditional markets, following auctioneers, buyers and sellers, beginning in Devon at Brixham fish auction. Stephen Tompkinson narrates

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion and Mark Stacey are off to Belgium, where a Victorian cane topper and an acupuncture model catch their eye, but it remains to be seen which is the most valuable

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of recipes from the BBC archives for party food, including Lorraine Pascale's white chocolate lollipops, seafood dishes by James Martin and Rachel Khoo's coq-au-vin on a stick

programme.title: Made in Great Britain
programme.desc: Steph McGovern pays a visit to York, where chocolate was transformed from a luxury drink for the rich into the nation's favourite treat. She meets up with Yorkshireman and chocolatier Paul A Young, who guides her through the history of the city's association with the food. Chocolate started as an expensive luxury drink, until the manufacture of a solid product became possible. Initially, hand-decorated chocolates with exotic flavours were sold to wealthy customers before, in the 1930s, chocolate became available for all as in the form of the Kit Kat bar

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Flower's pups find themselves alone during a storm when their uncle Youssarian abandons his babysitting duties. Meanwhile, the Lazulis try to expand their territory and move a little too close for the Whiskers' liking

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques experts Caroline Hawley and Michael Baggott find objects at the University of Nottingham to take off to auction. Caroline comes across a jacket worn by Nottingham-born Olympic ice skater Jayne Torvill, and Michael gets excited by a 17th-century Bible that is hiding a secret. Host Paul Martin travels to north Nottinghamshire to visit the Portland Collection, which is one of the largest aristocratic art collections in Britain

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Gemma Cairney, Tim Key, Gabby Logan and Jeff Stelling go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick meets a family who make some of the best Gouda cheese in Britain, and uses their creations to make a tortilla dish with caramelised apples and onions. In the fishing village of Mevagissey, he discovers the origins of the sea shanty and the history of the pilchard fishing industry, when shoals of pilchards were so large they would attract killer whales

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: Competition is fierce as the ring-netters of Newlyn go on the hunt for sardines. Also known as pilchards, they were once a staple of the Cornish fishing industry, caught using drift nets by the tens-of-thousands, then packed in salt and sent to Italy. But demand waned and the drift net fishery, once the mainstay of the Cornish fleet, faded away in the 1960s. In recent years, the humble pilchard has been rebranded as the Cornish sardine, and the hundreds of drift netters have been replaced by just 15 hi-tech ring-netters - and the sardine is once more a highly-prized and lucrative catch

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a sock factory in Leicester that produces one and a half million socks annually, while Cherry Healey discovers the science behind smelly feet and tests brands of socks to see how they cope with the problem. Ruth Goodman looks back at how socks became a fashion statement in the 1980s and a vital piece of military kit in the First World War

programme.title: Cheetah Family & Me
programme.desc: Part one of two. Wildlife film-maker Gordon Buchanan gets close to wild cheetah in the Kalahari, experiencing first-hand the very real dangers these beautiful big cats face. The programme follows two females, each with a litter of cubs, as they navigate a harsh environment, dangerous wildlife and dwindling prey

programme.title: A Teacher
programme.desc: For Eric's 18th birthday, Claire takes him away in secret for the weekend. Away from his friends and family when he should be with them, Eric is blissfully unaware of just how far down a path of destruction he has been taken. Later, Claire makes a decision that will finally bring her crashing into reality

programme.title: A Teacher
programme.desc: With their secret out, Eric has to contend with the whole school knowing and Claire is forced reluctantly to confront the reality of what she has done. With neither of them thinking straight, a bad situation quickly takes a turn for the worse

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events

programme.title: Celebrity: A 21st Century Story
programme.desc: The documentary charting the transformation of celebrity examines how people capitalised on the digital revolution in order to sidestep the traditional routes to fame. When the old tabloid machinery began to lose power, celebrities made the move to the digital press - and soon after pop acts were making a buzz on the internet. Meanwhile, structured reality shows dominated the younger demographic - and all of these things came together through social media. All of a sudden there was a new way of becoming famous

programme.title: Celebrity: A 21st Century Story
programme.desc: Having learned how to sidestep the conventional media machinery, celebrities acquired the tools take charge of their own narrative on social media. In 2016, actress Meghan Markle became Prince Harry's girlfriend. They publicly confirmed their relationship and swiftly denounced the press. Meanwhile, Donald Trump became US President, taking everything he'd learned from business and media to his role, including catchphrases and shock tactics. Ed Balls showed his moves on the Strictly dancefloor, Rachel Johnson entered the Big Brother house, and her father Stanley the I'm a Celebrity jungle, so while celebrities started playing politics, politicians were seemingly behaving more and more like celebrities

programme.title: Last Woman on Earth with Sara Pascoe
programme.desc: The comedian travels the world to meet people doing rare crafts and jobs that are rapidly becoming lost skills, and tries to pick up their unusual talents. Her journey begins in Cuba, where she finds many professions are being put at risk by increased commercialism and meets a mattress magician, a cigar factory reader and a family who climb giant coconut trees to make a local sweet

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The first of two special Christmas episodes of the culinary challenge, in which eight finalists and semi-finalists from past years return to face a festive test of their skills. The first episode features Danny Parker from the 2014 series, 2017's Louisa Ellis, 2009's Marianne Lumb, and 2018 semi-finalist William Chilila. Monica Galetti sets them the task of making festive canapés, before they move on to the dreaded skills test and a seasonally themed two-course menu

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Claire Duffy wanted the best in life for her young family, especially youngest daughter Abbie, who had recently received a cystic fibrosis diagnosis. She was sure that the warm weather and outdoor lifestyle of New Zealand would do wonders for Abbie's health, but had to convince home bird husband Russell that moving halfway across the world was a viable option. Two years later, have they made the leap, or was the pull of home just too strong? Nicki Chapman catches up with the family to find out

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Hexham's fortnightly market brings hope of selling some lamb and beef to farmer Andrew and his daughter Emma, and Lee and Shelly are selling produce from their Tamworth pigs

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Nadeem Islam meets Lynn Stewart-Taylor, who takes him on a tour of Jersey which he samples local cuisine, history and culture. With voiceover

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson shares the rhythms and rituals of her kitchen and reveals the inspiration behind many of her recipes. She begins with her take on bhorta, a dish of fried and mashed vegetables mainly associated with Bangladesh, to which she introduces a very British - but very familiar - ingredient. After creating a warm, spicy and aromatic dish of lamb shanks with wide noodles, Nigella uses very ripe bananas to make a rich and gooey chocolate tahini pudding, and then goes on to create a warming curry with a surprising ingredient

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Rob Beckett hosts the quiz in which a teacher, an assistant engineer and a legal writer must choose between working together to pool their knowledge or going it alone

programme.title: Coast and Country Auctions
programme.desc: Cameras explore the Welsh Cob Autumn Sale at Builth Wells, and follow a few of the attendees selling horses for different reasons. Narrated by Stephen Tompkinson

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Antiques experts Christina Trevanion and Mark Stacey face off at an auction in Sevenoaks, where Christina plays catch-up and Mark falls in love with a romantic painting

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of recipes from the BBC archives, ideal to draw upon when planning the perfect picnic. The menu includes Paul Hollywood's pork pies with quail eggs, a superb sandwich from James Martin and Ainsley's own mustard, bacon and onion tarts

programme.title: Made in Great Britain
programme.desc: Though Luton is now famous for its airport, it once led the world in hat making and even today, there are 17 hat-making businesses in the making extraordinary creations for many high-profile clients, including the royal family. Here, the makers learn how the hat industry was transformed from a small rural craft into a global fashion phenomenon, and after trying their hand in various production tasks involved in the making of hats through history are given complete free rein to produce a hat that expresses their personality. Presented by Steph McGovern

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: The Whiskers are caught raiding their rivals' burrow and forced to beat a hasty retreat - but there are dire consequences. Meanwhile, Flower learns one of her daughters has a secret. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Some of the best unseen finds from the show's valuation days from around the country, including visits to the Milestones Museum in Basingstoke and Ragely Hall in Warwickshire

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Gemma Cairney, Tim Key, Gabby Logan and Jeff Stelling go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in trivia-based games across the week, with a winner declared each Friday

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick meets chef Tom Adams, who runs Combeshead Farm, a leading field-to-fork restaurant where they grow and produce all of their own food, from sourdough bread to pork pies, as well as rearing a small herd of Mangalitsa pigs, a hairy, old-world breed that produces exquisite hams. Rick tries out a new recipe - pork chops with a sloe berry sauce - before moving on to the village of Rock, where his son Jack holds a clam bake on the shore of the Camel Estuary

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: A brother and sister team fish for oysters in a traditional wooden sailing boat on the Fal estuary - but this is not enough to support them, so now they plan to trade it in for a small trawler. Further down river in St Mawes, a veteran trawler captain reflects on a past brush with death and the changing face of his home town, as working boats give way to yachts

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: In Armadale, at the northernmost tip of the Scottish mainland, Storm Ciara is wreaking havoc for hill farmer Joyce. Meanwhile, Father and daughter team Frank and Georgia Hunter are also battling the storm on their mixed livestock farm in Cumbria, and when the storm ruptures a section of the mains water pipe, their water supply is cut off. Elsewhere, Helen and Graham deal with a missing calf

programme.title: Cheetah Family & Me
programme.desc: Part two of two. Wildlife film-maker Gordon Buchanan gets close to wild cheetah in the Kalahari, experiencing first-hand the very real dangers these beautiful big cats face. With the help of guides Richard Satekge and Julius Mkhize, this programme follows two female cheetah, each with a litter of cubs, as they navigate harsh environments, dangerous wildlife and dwindling prey

programme.title: A Teacher
programme.desc: Eric has made it to University of Texas and the Omega Kappa Beta fraternity with Logan's older brother Cody. But the rumours of what happened between him and Claire are rampant

programme.title: A Teacher
programme.desc: Claire emerges from her jail time needing to rebuild the life she destroyed - reluctantly reunited with her father and sleeping in her brother's basement

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events

programme.title: Last Woman on Earth with Sara Pascoe
programme.desc: The comedian travels the world to meet people doing rare crafts and jobs that are rapidly becoming lost skills, and tries to pick up their unusual talents. Her journey begins in Cuba, where she finds many professions are being put at risk by increased commercialism and meets a mattress magician, a cigar factory reader and a family who climb giant coconut trees to make a local sweet

programme.title: Last Woman on Earth with Sara Pascoe
programme.desc: The comedian's world tour of unusual jobs takes her to Georgia, a country bordered by Russia and the Black Sea that has gone through a drastic period of modernisation in the last 10 years - leaving behind some very rare trades with centuries of history. Among the last practitioners of distinctive Georgian skills are a gunsmith, a polyphonic singer and a guide at the Stalin Museum

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Nadeem Islam meets Lynn Stewart-Taylor, who takes him on a tour of Jersey which he samples local cuisine, history and culture. With voiceover

programme.title: Marcus Rashford: Feeding Britain's Children
programme.desc: A look at the Manchester United and England star's campaign calling for children from poor families in England to get free meals during the school holidays. The 23 year-old striker speaks openly about his personal connection with hunger, his experiences of going without food as a child and the sacrifices his family had to make. The film also hears from those closest to him, including his mum Mel, his brothers Dwaine and Dane and his friends, while Marcus meets some of the families directly affected by the issue

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Four of the most memorable professional chefs from past series - Steve Barringer, Exose Grant Lopo-Ndigna, Oli Martin and Olivia Burt - are invited to return to the MasterChef kitchen to compete in a festive-themed special. First, the chefs have the chance to relive the much-feared Skills Test, and catch up with judges on how their careers have progressed since taking part in the competition. Later, they are tasked to create a seasonally themed two-course menu in two-and-half hours to impress the judges

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

While he thought a move to New Zealand would be a great idea, he needed to convince wife Amanda and son Aaron, who didn't have the same enthusiasm. Could a trial week give them a glimpse into what they might be able to achieve? Three years on, where are the Prices based now?

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two sets of farmers - Jack and Nina and Andy and Tim - hope to make a splash with their rapeseed oils and sauces at a market in the small town of Axbridge, Somerset

programme.title: 12 Puppies and Us
programme.desc: Shih tzu-pug cross Miska moves on to a houseboat with new owner Scarlett. After signs that Miska is adapting well to her new home, Scarlett takes her to work, but she is shocked when the dog ignores her and starts yapping at her colleagues in the open-plan office. In Surrey, the Summerscale-Heards continue training St Bernard pup Woody to offer assistance to five-year-old Dexter, and in West Yorkshire, Phoebe the cavapoo joins retirees Linda and Andrew - only to start nipping and jumping up

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Quiz show hosted by Rob Beckett, in which contestants Yasmin, Michael and Dubem must choose between working together or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves

programme.title: Coast and Country Auctions
programme.desc: A visit to Exeter Livestock Market, the biggest agricultural auction in the region, where a team of auctioneers can sell more than 3,000 animals in about four hours

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Antiques experts Christina Trevanion and Mark Stacey continue their rivalry at Newark Antique Fair, where he buys some kitsch poodles while she chooses a silver piggy

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces recipes that can be prepared quickly, including Nigel Slater's herb butter squid, James Martin's icebox cake and Mary Berry's chocolate tray bake

programme.title: Made in Great Britain
programme.desc: Steph McGovern leads the makers through the ages from the 17th century to the present day, revealing how Stoke-on-Trent dominated the pottery industry, transforming from a collection of rural villages to a city that became the world capital of ceramics. The makers are first tasked with making elementary butter pots, before moving on to an oblong-shaped tea canister, decorated with blue cobalt pigment. They also study the invention of Bone China, use a 'jiggering' machine to shape clay into a plate and visit celebrated pottery company Wedgwood

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Trouble brews among the meerkats as the impact of two simultaneous pregnancies makes its mark. Youssarian is left to babysit Flower's second litter of the year, while Mozart tries to assert her authority on the Whiskers. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People in Weston-super-Mare bring their valuables along to the Grand Pier for valuation by experts Jonathan Pratt and Thomas Plant. Plus, presenter Paul Martin discovers more about the seaside pier itself

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Gemma Cairney, Tim Key, Gabby Logan and Jeff Stelling test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick discovers an unusual superstition and a rare type of Norman castle in Launceston, before meeting musicians Graham Fitkin and Ruth Wall in the far west of Cornwall

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: A look at the smaller boats that fish off Cornwall's coast, often with only a single crew member. Despite being a sustainable method of fishing, this traditional approach is coming under threat from industrialisation, a lack of new talent taking up the trade, and declining stocks of mackerel and other once common fish. In Penberth, James is the only fisherman under 65 - and since traditional methods rely on boats supporting each other, he may have to give up when the older generation retires. On the Helford River, a veteran fisherman shares his concerns for the fish processing industry if Britain leaving the EU leads to a decline in foreign labour

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: In the Solent, one the busiest stretches of water in the UK, reports come in of a missing kite surfer. The crew of Lymington lifeboat station start searching, but as the hours pass they begin to fear the worst. Twenty miles to the west, the Poole crew are called out to save a father and daughter from drowning as they are swept out to sea, and at Chiswick, one of the few RNLI stations with a crew on permanent standby 24/7, an unusual casualty in the Thames leaves the crew attempting to save not just one but nine lives

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig asks questions on animals beginning with the letter R, with Tom Allen, Ed Gamble, Maggie Aderin-Pocock and Alan Davies trying to come up with interesting answers - while avoiding the most obvious ones

programme.title: A Teacher
programme.desc: Eric and Claire are slowly forced to confront the full extent of the damage that their relationship has inflicted on them and everyone around them

programme.title: A Teacher
programme.desc: Ten years on, Eric is working with emotionally vulnerable children, while Claire has children of her own. Returning to Austin for his high school reunion, Eric is brought back, after far too long, to his old friends and to Alison. It feels good, but another reunion, inevitably, is soon to follow

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: MasterChef judge Gregg Wallace and mathematician Hannah Fry take over a restaurant and invite five celebrities to enjoy a dinner party with a difference, where they will be scored on the carbon footprint of every dish they choose. Diners Sara Pascoe, Amol Rajan, Nikki Fox, Desiree Burch and Matthew Fort choose from a menu of tantalising treats, each of which tells its own environmental story

programme.title: The Noughties
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon is joined by singer Alexandra Burke and comedian Chris Ramsey to take a look at the year's highlights, including Barack Obama becoming America's first black president and Gwyneth Paltrow's launch of lifestyle brand Goop. Alexandra also reflects on winning The X Factor that year and how it felt to share a stage with Beyoncé

programme.title: Celebrity: A 21st Century Story
programme.desc: The world of celebrity has been transformed since the turn of the century, driven by some of the most dramatic technological and cultural developments in recent times. The first episode examines how the Beckhams changed the model of the celebrity power couple, while the rise of reality TV led to a gigantic influx of people gaining overnight fame, fed by a press that saw an easy source of material. The programme also examines how leaked sex tapes changed the nature of celebrity scandal

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Winter Walks
programme.desc: Regional series in which celebrities explore landscapes in Yorkshire and Cumbria. Lemn Sissay walks along ancient drovers' roads in Cumbria's Dentdale, meeting locals, including a farmer who collects vintage tractors and a lone bagpiper, and making a challenging climb to England's highest mainline train station

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a picture of a snow-covered forest, withn veautiful leafless trees and a texture you can almost feel

programme.title: Inside Obama's White House
programme.desc: Four-part series giving an account of Barack Obama's successes and failures, as told by his inner circle and chief adversaries, including the story of how he tried to reshape America. The election of the first African American President marked an era of hope, but what happened instead was economic collapse, minimal cooperation from the Republican opposition, and the rise of a populist anti-big government Tea Party. The first edition focuses on how Obama crashed a private meeting of fellow heads of state to persuade the Chinese to tackle climate change at the Copenhagen summit, as well as his struggle to shut down Guantanamo

programme.title: Tutankhamun in Colour
programme.desc: Egyptologist Elizabeth Frood takes viewers back to 1922 to experience the discovery of Tutankhamun's tomb as it happened, with the help of colourised photographs and film footage. The documentary follows the story from the first encounter between Howard Carter and Lord Carnarvon, the discovery of a vast chamber filled with ancient relics to the opening of the burial chamber itself, and reveals how the discoveries made changed how the boy king and Egypt as a nation were viewed around the world

programme.title: Murder 24/7
programme.desc: A man is arrested for the murder of Courtney Valentine-Brown, but during his interview, he names another as being the killer. This sparks a manhunt to locate him, but the search is against the clock, as detectives must find the second suspect before it becomes necessary to either charge or release the first

programme.title: Light and Dark
programme.desc: The first of a two-part documentary in which Professor Jim Al-Khalili examines how some of history's greatest minds began to uncover the secrets of the universe by using and manipulating light. In the first edition, the presenter considers the significance of Greek mathematician Euclid's landmark discovery that light moves in straight lines. He then moves on to Renaissance Italy, where Galileo Galilei unlocked the potential of telescopes in locating Earth's true place within the cosmos, before assessing the implications of Danish astronomer Ole Roemer's groundbreaking finding - that light moves at a finite speed

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: With Gilou facing the possibility of prison, Laure's team try to recover their reputation by taking on a case that no-one else wants - the murder of an identified minor in a run-down part of town. The return of the French detective drama, starring Caroline Proust. In French

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Gilou makes headway as an informer and gets close to Cisco, a dangerous convict coming to the end of his sentence who is being monitored by the police. Josephine Karlsson meets Souleymane, the 13-year-old Moroccan boy accused of the murder Laure and Ali are investigating. In French

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a picture of a snow-covered forest, withn veautiful leafless trees and a texture you can almost feel

programme.title: Murder 24/7
programme.desc: Documentary following murder investigations by Essex police, from the critical first few days right through to arrest and conviction. It begins with the first of three programmes charting the inquiry into the fatal stabbing of 36-year-old Courtney Valentine-Brown in Southend-on-Sea, Essex, as a team of detectives and specialists work around the clock to catch the killers

programme.title: Winter Walks
programme.desc: Celebrities explore landscapes in Yorkshire and Cumbria. Simon Armitage takes a coastal walk along Yorkshire's enchanting and historic frontier with the North Sea. Starting in Ravenscar, Simon follows a disused railway line, passing former quarries and brickworks, before taking a beach walk from Boggle Hole to the picture-perfect Robin Hood's Bay

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: Bob Ross captures the serenity of a snow-covered farmhouse and barn on a cold day in January

programme.title: Queen Victoria's Children
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the reign of the monarch through her relationships with her husband and their children, using letters, diaries, memoirs and journals to bring the subject and characters to life. The first episode focuses on Victoria and Prince Albert's tempestuous marriage, their attempts to engineer the upbringing of their children and their efforts to save the monarchy by projecting a modern image of the royal family

programme.title: Lucy Worsley's Royal Palace Secrets
programme.desc: The historian goes behind closed doors at three treasure-filled royal palaces to uncover how each building has shaped a monarchy and a nation. With exclusive access to the palaces' most secret places, she uncovers the dramatic events that changed the course of British history, from might and intimidation at the Tower of London and the rise and fall of absolute monarchy at Hampton Court, to the modern face of royalty at Kensington Palace

programme.title: Murder 24/7
programme.desc: The investigation into the murder of Courtney Valentine-Brown nears a conclusion. With one suspect outstanding, one on bail and one in custody, can the team of detectives and specialists secure enough evidence to charge them with murder?

programme.title: Seven Ages of Starlight
programme.desc: Leading scientists including solar physicist Dr Helen Mason and astronomer Dr Francisco Diego explain the story of the stars, revealing how they have transformed man's understanding of the universe and helped to shape life on Earth. The programme shows the part the celestial bodies have played in the evolution of the cosmos, from so-called red giants awaiting to explode to supernovae and black holes. Narrated by Rupert Graves

She was sure that the warm weather and outdoor lifestyle of New Zealand would do wonders for Abbie's health, but had to convince home bird husband Russell that moving halfway across the world was a viable option. Two years later, have they made the leap, or was the pull of home just too strong? Nicki Chapman catches up with the family to find out

programme.title: Traces

Sarah's report has some damning things to say about the safety of the building, but soon feeling overwhelmed by the police interest she wonders what she's started. Emma is cautioned by DI McKinven to stop sleuthing, as the stars align to put Daniel Macafee in her path - for whom she feels a deep connection. It later transpires that Daniel harbours a guilty conscience as he was employed by the company that refurbished Secrets. Crime drama by Amelia Bullmore, starring Molly Windsor, Laura Fraser and Jennifer Spence

programme.desc: Documentary exploring the hidden world of night-time workers - those unsung heroes who occupy the graveyard shift in a wide range of jobs. This invisible army works in the dark to make sure the day runs like clockwork

programme.desc: In the wake of the coronavirus pandemic, the health market has exploded with products promising to boost, support or supercharge our natural defences and protect you from infectious bacteria and viruses. programme.title: I Am Greta

Starting with her one-person school strike for climate justice outside the Swedish Parliament, the film follows Greta - a shy student with Asperger's - as she rises to prominence, and her galvanising global impact as she sparks school strikes around the world

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The Caribbean-set whodunit returns for its 10th anniversary series, which promises more murders and the return of one or two familiar faces. A TV news presenter is found dead in her pool at home. DI Neville Parker is convinced her co-host is responsible. But given he was live on air at the time, how could he have done it? The return of an experienced sergeant to the island could be just what Neville needs to crack the case

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Dan Walker and Louise Minchin

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: Stories of holiday-makers left stranded at the airport because their Covid-19 test results came back too late, and people who arrived at a hotel only to discover it was closed

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit two neighbouring cottages in Derbyshire, a terrace in Bolton and a semi in Leeds before they are sold under the hammer

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Claire Duffy wanted the best in life for her young family, especially youngest daughter Abbie, who had recently received a cystic fibrosis diagnosis. She was sure that the warm weather and outdoor lifestyle of New Zealand would do wonders for Abbie's health, but had to convince home bird husband Russell that moving halfway across the world was a viable option. Two years later, have they made the leap, or was the pull of home just too strong? Nicki Chapman catches up with the family to find out

programme.title: Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A pair of pyjamas reveal the shocking true identity of an attacker who left his victim for dead, and a bright yellow baseball cap puts a daylight robber behind bars

programme.title: Coronavirus: Scotland's Response
programme.desc: Live coverage of the Scottish Government's Briefing with reaction, expert analysis and the very latest updates from Scotland and beyond on the pandemic

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A new musical director for the bell-ringers of St Mary's leads to discord, disharmony and the blood-curdling sound of murder. Mystery, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers cook comfort food dishes straight from the oven, including a new take on steak and chips and sticky gingerbread

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a house hunter from Surrey to find a new home in Pembrokeshire, and finds her a remote period house with spectacular views within her budget of £900,000

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Hexham's fortnightly market brings hope of selling some lamb and beef to farmer Andrew and his daughter Emma, and Lee and Shelly are selling produce from their Tamworth pigs

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents the programme in which five dealers bid against one another to buy a pedal car, an Edwardian card table, a quirky voice box and a mystery pot

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Jack sets his plan in motion and manages to negotiate an advantageous deal, meaning he soon has good news for Denise. Stacey is in for a surprise, Whitney has a choice to make and Ruby has to come up with an explanation for Martin. Ash has advice for Sonia after she ends another exhausting shift at the hospital

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: A shocking discovery is made about Poppy's tormentor, while Alex tracks Paul back to his house as the search for Lenny intensifies. Ellie worries that Caitlin has given up on any chance of freedom, and Bob tries to come to terms with Kim's new relationship

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Sacha buries his head in the sand when Beka is taken ill on New Year's Eve. Kian is on edge as Sarah Jane avoids his calls, and his stash is running out. He tries to convince Jac he is fine when she worries that their relationship is the problem. It is total chaos on AAU, and the F1s are daunted when Hanssen leaves them alone to face their first New Year's night shift

programme.title: Traces
programme.desc: As Kathy and Sarah work on their reports for the Secrets nightclub fire, the emerging picture is that two of the deaths were accidental, but the third victim was dead before the fire started. Sarah's report has some damning things to say about the safety of the building, but soon feeling overwhelmed by the police interest she wonders what she's started. Emma is cautioned by DI McKinven to stop sleuthing, as the stars align to put Daniel Macafee in her path - for whom she feels a deep connection. It later transpires that Daniel harbours a guilty conscience as he was employed by the company that refurbished Secrets. Crime drama by Amelia Bullmore, starring Molly Windsor, Laura Fraser and Jennifer Spence

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: After discovering the network's plan and learning they will not be going to America, David and Michael are dismayed to learn who will be going in their place. The guys are also upset to find out how well Simon is doing without them

programme.title: Accused: A Mother on Trial
programme.desc: Rex searches for evidence to implicate Kennard in the crime. Details of Ashley and Kennard's past reveal why she might be covering up for him. Kennard finally breaks his silence

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: Contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing a cash prize. Helping them this time round are Stacey Dooley, Roman Kemp, Omid Djalili, Judy Murray, Owain Wyn Evans, Kate Humble and Vikkstar

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Dan Walker and Louise Minchin

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville ask who is responsible when valuables are stolen from a coach. Plus, the bride and groom left waiting at the airport

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell view properties in Clapham, London, Maidstone, Kent and Whitehaven, Cumbria

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Lee Price was a huge fan of the Down Under lifestyle, having fallen in love with the place after getting married there. While he thought a move to New Zealand would be a great idea, he needed to convince wife Amanda and son Aaron, who didn't have the same enthusiasm. Could a trial week give them a glimpse into what they might be able to achieve? Three years on, where are the Prices based now?

programme.title: Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a discarded scrap of paper found in a household bin helped convict a killer, and a distinctive mole holds the key to tracking down a dangerous and predatory attacker

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning and Christina Trevanion present from the Shropshire town of Oswestry, where two teams shop for antiques with the help of experts Kate Bliss and Ochuko Ojiri. Christina also visits one of the most historically important buildings in Britain, a flax mill in Shrewsbury that can lay claim to being the world's first skyscraper

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Poor weather plagues Bunty and Mrs McCarthy's picnic. Meanwhile, Father Brown must intervene when it appears that a dark spirit has murderous intent

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers give a traditional pudding a new look, reveal the secrets behind the ultimate fish cake and offer their take on Teesside dish chicken parmo

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: With a top budget of £560,000, Jules Hudson assists a retired couple in their bid to move to Cornwall, where they hope to get involved in the local community

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two sets of farmers - Jack and Nina and Andy and Tim - hope to make a splash with their rapeseed oils and sauces at a market in the small town of Axbridge, Somerset

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers hosts as five dealers bid against each other for items brought in by the public, including odd medical models, a knife sharpener, a drinks cabinet and a toy milk cart

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants have to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present the magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents a programme in which five dealers bid against one another to buy intriguing items brought in by members of the public. The opening edition features an unusual item of hairdressing memorabilia, something salvaged from a tip, a classic piece of industrial furniture and a beloved toy

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The second of two festive shows sees The Vivienne, Lesley Joseph, Sabrina Grant and Sally Phillips competing to show off their dress-making skills. Judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young ask them to make a child's party skirt and then turn old clothes into a recognisable pantomime character in the transformation challenge. They then create a made-to-measure party dress that evokes memories of a particular New Year's Eve - with surprising results

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: In the wake of the coronavirus pandemic, the health market has exploded with products promising to boost, support or supercharge our natural defences and protect you from infectious bacteria and viruses. This programme offers a guide to strengthening the immune system during cold and flu season, and examines some of the so-called 'miracle' products, superfoods and supplements that claim to help

programme.title: I Am Greta
programme.desc: The story of teenage climate activist Greta Thunberg, told through never-before-seen footage of the girl who has become the voice of a generation. Starting with her one-person school strike for climate justice outside the Swedish Parliament, the film follows Greta - a shy student with Asperger's - as she rises to prominence, and her galvanising global impact as she sparks school strikes around the world

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Rebecca Ellis had always dreamt of a move to Australia, inspired by her grandfather's tales of a beautiful and friendly country. Could a week in Brisbane help them come round to Rebecca's way of thinking? Three years since we last caught up with them, where do they call home - Australia or the UK?

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: More celebrity interviews and topical debate from a female perspective

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson helps members of the public in Grimsby sort the treasures from their trash, including a teddy bear, a miniature tea set and a collection of spoons

programme.title: Lingo
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Adil Ray in which a couple, a mother and son, and a father and son use their powers of deduction to work out words that appear in the Lingo grids and Puzzlewords. The last remaining duo have the opportunity to win the money they've banked earlier in the show to potentially double their winnings, or leaving with nothing

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants James, Karen, Jerry and Belinda pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Liv is in danger, Vinny panics, and Jamie warns Will

programme.title: South Africa with Gregg Wallace
programme.desc: The MasterChef presenter explores the country's most famous and best-loved landscapes and experiences, beginning by going on safari at the Amakhala Game Reserve in the Eastern Cape. After a drive through the reserve, he camps out overnight and tastes his first poike - a traditional South African stew - cooked over a campfire. In the morning, Gregg joins vet Will Fowlds and his team as they care for a wild leopard

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire: The Million Pound Question
programme.desc: A look back at the infamous game of Charles Ingram, the man the press later dubbed the Coughing Major. Jeremy Clarkson and Chris Tarrant are joined by former celebrity contestants and other big winners to recount their memories of Charles' unforgettable journey to the million pound question. Plus, Laurence Llwellyn-Bowen recalls getting a second chance to win a million

programme.title: Beat the Chasers
programme.desc: Contestants from across the UK take on Jenny 'The Vixen' Ryan, Anne 'The Governess' Hegerty, Shaun 'The Dark Destroyer' Wallace, Mark 'The Beast' Labbett and Paul 'The Sinnerman' Sinha, hoping to win big money. Bradley Walsh hosts

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Rock On, Tommy: The Bobby Ball Story
programme.desc: In a tribute to one of the UK's most popular entertainers, friends, family and co-stars celebrate the life and work of comedian and actor Bobby Ball. It reveals how a factory welder from Oldham went on to become one of the best loved stars on British television. It's narrated by Bobby's colleague of 57 years, Tommy Cannon

programme.desc: The MasterChef presenter explores the country's most famous and best-loved landscapes and experiences, beginning by going on safari at the Amakhala Game Reserve in the Eastern Cape. In the morning, Gregg joins vet Will Fowlds and his team as they care for a wild leopard

Plus, Laurence Llwellyn-Bowen recalls getting a second chance to win a million

programme.desc: Surreal comedy from the mind of Harry Hill, inspired by viewers' clips, featuring the horrors of the Harry Styles Finishing School, the terrifying sight of Beyonce without her make-up, and Sarah Millican fighting for her fame with her trusty nunchaku

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers featuring celebrity lookalikes, with kids using Brian May's scalp as a pinata, Orlando Bloom showing off his new truss and Mumford & Sons revealing how they keep in touch with the working classes

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
