programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Joe Thomas grapples grumpily with some awkward hand-wear, Sian Gibson seems to find proof that she may be psychic and Iain Stirling shows some tasty shortbread who's boss

programme.title: First Dates Hotel
programme.desc: Manchester likely lads Adam and Dean check in for dates with best friends Kate and Charlotte. For 26-year-old Kate, her date with Adam is fraught with self-doubt as she was bullied as a child. Meanwhile, 39-year-old sign language interpreter Peter was born to deaf parents and didn't learn to talk until he was seven. He also seems to struggle with the language of love when some lewd comments threaten to send his dream woman packing

programme.title: Angel
programme.desc: The gang learns the Beast is working for an even more powerful entity. Lilah tries to make a deal with Angelus, while Connor and Gunn go in search of a method to restore Angel's soul. Supernatural drama, starring David Boreanaz

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny and Stevie pitch a deal to a nearby golf course, while Moira tries to get out of Twyla's murder mystery party. Sitcom, starring Eugene Levy

programme.title: How I Met Your Mother

Meanwhile, Ted and Cassie try to enjoy a trip to a lighthouse, and Marshall and Daphne must deal with a stowaway on their road trip. American sitcom, starring Josh Radnor and Alyson Hannigan

programme.title: How I Met Your Mother
programme.desc: Robin is upset to discover her mother cannot attend the wedding. Guest starring Bryan Cranston

programme.title: Supernanny US

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Mercedes plans to unearth the mole in her family. Meanwhile, Celeste can see how happy Toby is as he prepares for his date with Cleo

programme.title: Hollyoaks

programme.title: How I Met Your Mother
programme.desc: Barney becomes convinced he can reunite his parents after seeing them together for the first time in years - but does not realise his brother James has something else in mind. programme.title: How I Met Your Mother
programme.desc: Marshall tries to get baby Marvin to sleep while they are stuck in traffic during their long journey to Farhampton for the wedding, and devises three rhyming bedtime stories based around the gang's many exploits. American sitcom, guest starring James Van Der Beek

programme.title: Supernanny US

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: After more than five years of dating, Sheldon and Amy spend their first night together, while Leonard, Howard and Raj must decide who will take their extra Star Wars movie ticket. programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Competitive juices are flowing as Jake and the squad compete in the second Jimmy Jab Games - a series of eccentric races and contests held in the precinct building

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny and Leonard announce that they are moving in together, prompting Sheldon to revisit 2003, a much simpler time before he had met either of them. Howard and Raj form a band to play at the comic-book store, only to fall out over musical differences

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The boys want to become more sporty, so Barry Kripke leads them in a fencing lesson, while Penny, Amy and Bernadette help Stuart, who wants to attract more female customers to the comic-book store

programme.title: Scrubs
programme.desc: Carla goes into labour, but an unforeseen mishap means Turk may end up missing the birth. JD and Kim contemplate their relationship and the future of their baby, prompting Jordan to reveal a secret from her past. Starring Zach Braff and Judy Reyes

programme.title: Scrubs
programme.desc: Elliot tries to persuade Dr Turner, a surgeon with 30 years' experience under his belt, that an operation could kill him. programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Part one of two. Joe's dying grandmother begs him to come and visit one last time on her birthday, and to bring his wife Tiffany along. He had failed to tell his family about the divorce, so Mel is forced to take a road trip to New Jersey and pose as his ex-wife at the party. Starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

Family comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: In an attempt to solve an international jewel theft case, Peralta and Diaz are forced to work with a pair of Swedish detectives and things get very competitive, very quickly. Santiago and Jeffords offer to help Gina prepare for an examination, and Captain Holt enlists Boyle as his squash partner for an important tournament. Anders Holm and Riki Lindhome guest star

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Peralta, Boyle and Gina get involved in a Die Hard-type hostage situation while doing some off-duty shopping on Christmas Eve, so Jeffords is forced to leave his family celebration to try to save his squad. Santiago attempts to prove that she's just as tough as Captain Holt and Diaz. Comedy, starring Andy Samberg

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.desc: The prodigy studies his family for a psychology project. Thanksgiving dinner ends up in an argument when Georgie Sr is offered a job in Oklahoma. Comedy prequel to The Big Bang Theory, starring Iain Armitage

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.desc: When the young genius learns that people with stunted childhoods can turn into social outcasts, he tries to act like a stereotypical child. Meemaw and Dr Sturgis' relationship is tested when she teaches him how to drive

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny and Bernadette go to San Diego for a pharmaceutical sales convention, leaving Howard in charge of the children. When Sheldon finds a book containing science experiments to carry out with children, the guys and Amy make a day of it, using little Michael and Halley as test subjects

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon and Amy's academic rivals embark on a publicity tour, so the couple try to get the Nobel laureates on their side. Halley's fear of the dark leads to opportunity and conflict for Howard, Bernadette and Stuart

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: John Paul returns to the village after the events of New Year's Eve, and is shocked to hear about everything he has missed with Silas and Mercedes

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: The next phase of the experiment sees the first six couples head off on their honeymoons, and while it proves to be smooth sailing for some, it is a bumpy road for others

programme.title: Dodgeball: A True Underdog Story (2004)
programme.desc: The struggling owner of a run-down gym is forced to take drastic action to prevent an arrogant fitness guru buying the business out from under him - and enlists his customers to form a dodgeball team in the hope of triumphing in the US championships. Sporting comedy, starring Ben Stiller, Vince Vaughn and Christine Taylor

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Amy pulls out all the stops as she hosts a traditional Victorian Christmas Eve dinner for the friends. Meanwhile, Raj's father comes to visit, and the guys do their best to free a bird they accidentally let into the university clean room. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Raj endures an anxious day as he worries that a space probe he helped design will not function properly, while Leonard and Sheldon agree to go clothes shopping with Penny and Amy. Comedy, starring Kunal Nayyar, Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: Robot Chicken

programme.title: Rick and Morty
programme.desc: The duo find themselves on a space train that is one big narrative device, filled with weird passengers telling increasingly ridiculous anthology stories

programme.title: Taskmaster

programme.title: Angel

programme.title: Schitt's Creek

programme.title: How I Met Your Mother

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp looks back to the 1970s for vintage inspiration in this bite-sized episode. The presenter creates crocheted jellyfish and a colourful felt rug with the help of two of Britain's most gifted craftspeople

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Retired couple Roger and Obie Pearce want a slice of the Spanish good life. Obie has never been to Spain before, and Roger hasn't visited since he was in the army 40 years ago, but they are determined to relocate to Almeria where Roger's health could benefit from the warm climate. Ben Hillman sets out to find them their perfect home on a budget of £120,000

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole finds a treat for restorer Guy, and Simon O'Brien creates stunning table lamps from old theatre lights

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Max McMurdo head to Wales to see how can create the most profit on items, including an oil dispenser, fire grate, chaise longue and even a catamaran

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick visits Stephanie to help get the original chateau pizza oven working, while Annabelle and Justin return from holiday to find their chateau infested with bees

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton meets people as they bid to turn an empty plot of land into their dream home on an affordable budget. Adam and Vicki Nicholl have become tired of city life and decide to move back to the Hertfordshire village of Pirton where they grew up to build a barn-style property in the garden of Adam's mum's home. The pair are convinced they can save around £100,000 simply by not purchasing an existing property

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is Nateby Inn in Cumbria, where landlady Gaynor Walker is ready with a warm welcome. A trip to Gaynor's local brewery turns out to be a barrel of laughs when one guest beats another in a cask-rolling competition, before a broken cooker creates chaos in the kitchen the following morning

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners visit the Outfields Farm Bed and Breakfast in Ravensden, Bedfordshire, on their second stay, where proprietors Graham and Julia Ashley treat them to an afternoon of indoor sky-diving before hosting a dinner filled with surprises. The guests enjoy a good night's sleep, but one of the owners is expecting hassle at breakfast time

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to At Home in Chiswick, west London, where artist Bob Osbourne appears to take a rather laid-back approach to running his B&B. On arrival, guests are pleasantly surprised by their first impressions of the communal living space-come-mini art gallery - but it's not long before they are dishing the dirt

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition draws to a close at the Ammonite Lodge guesthouse in Chard, Somerset, where owners Andrew and Clare Ashmore hope to excite their guests with a game of skittles. During the final dinner of the week, the competitors discuss their first impressions of each other, and Bob and Julia share some amusing observations with the group

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners confront their critics face to face, and the winners are announced. Nateby Inn host Gaynor takes issue with Graham and Julia's reduced marks for cobwebs. The conversation becomes heated when Outfields Farm's owners tackle Gaynor's complaints in return, and Georgie jumps to their defence. Artist Bob Osborne has a long list of grievances he wants to address, and Julia is left cringing when her previous comments rebound on her

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: George meets a father-of-three building a campervan for less than £700, made from old CDs and vinyl records, and a single dad who is renovating an oil-rig escape pod to make a home for himself and sons on the water. The presenter also continues his German road trip with a visit to the Tyrolean mountains, where he finds a house that looks like a cross between a large bird and a UFO, and he and Will Hardie save a decrepit old wartime lookout shed to use as the basis for their floating beach hut

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Cameras follow adolescent babirusa Tolo, who lives with his devoted mother Sula. They are very close, enjoying mud baths and pedicures together. But Sula has a hoof problem so they must be separated for the vet to intervene, and keepers are concerned for Tolo's wellbeing in her absence. Meanwhile, laid-back rock hyraxes Daissie and her partner Nungu are expecting their eighth litter. With the birth imminent, the couple's life of ease could be a thing of the past

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: New owners Amy and Marc have big dreams to turn their 20-room chateau into a B&B and yoga retreat. They first need to replace their defunct sewage system and create a pipe network within the chateau's ancient walls. Meanwhile, musicians Ben and Billie start renovating their chateau's final untouched room - the old library - and restore the skylight complete with its original Victorian mechanism

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Tracy and Steve Fox want to build an 'urban shed' featuring two studios, three bedrooms and a double-height space with a walkway, made out of industrial-style materials including corrugated cement fibreboard and 20ft panels of polycarbonate sheeting. Having discovered an old milk yard tucked away behind some flats and shops in south-east London, their long-held desire to build a highly unconventional family home and workspace begins. But their choice of unorthodox materials proves challenging and costly. Kevin McCloud follows their progress

programme.title: Chatsworth House: A Great British Year
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes at the stately home belonging to the Duke and Duchess of Devonshire. In the first edition, the Covid-19 pandemic breaks out just as the house is about to be opened to the public for the year, sending staff racing to make the house safe for visitors in time

programme.title: Christmas at Highclere and Warwick Castles
programme.desc: Part one of two. A behind-the-scenes look at life in Highclere Castle - the setting of the popular television series and film Downton Abbey - as they deck their halls and prepare to celebrate Christmas in their own unique way. The programme follows the small team of dedicated staff who are tasked with creating seasonal cheer in the castle, as they race to get everything prepared in time to welcome small groups of guests for guided tours and tea, as well as a virtual gin cocktail party hosted by the Earl and Countess of Carnarvon

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Forty-year-old Tania is rushed to St George's with life-threatening injuries, having been knocked over while crossing a road, while semi-professional footballer Lilli arrives in A&E after being elbowed in the face during a match. Seventy-seven-year-old Edwin is rushed to resus bleeding from a tumour on his neck, and as the medical team examines the wound, his partner Jeanette explains how their romance blossomed in later life. When a maxillofacial specialist arrives to plan the next stage of Edwin's treatment, it forces his family to confront the very painful truth

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy words-and-numbers quiz, inviting Sean Lock and Rob Beckett to take on Jon Richardson and Claudia Winkleman. Nick Mohammed and Joe Wilkinson join wordsmith Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while maths whizz Rachel Riley looks after the numbers

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits some of the restaurants that he set out to save, including The Mixing Bowl, The Olde Stone Mill, Peter's and Campania. Have their owners pulled themselves out of their nightmares?

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Forty-year-old Tania is rushed to St George's with life-threatening injuries, having been knocked over while crossing a road, while semi-professional footballer Lilli arrives in A&E after being elbowed in the face during a match. Seventy-seven-year-old Edwin is rushed to resus bleeding from a tumour on his neck, and as the medical team examines the wound, his partner Jeanette explains how their romance blossomed in later life. When a maxillofacial specialist arrives to plan the next stage of Edwin's treatment, it forces his family to confront the very painful truth

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty investigates shock reports that changing sea temperatures mean squid and chips could one day replace fish and chips as the national dish. Kate Quilton looks into where multivitamins come from and how much we need them, while Matt Tebbutt visits the UK's leading clotted cream producer in Cornwall and an astonishing algae farm in the desert in Israel

programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp joins jewellery-maker Rachel Jeffrey to help a soon-to-be-married couple create their own wedding rings, and learns how gold can revive an old picture frame at little cost

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Ben and Kelly Jukes have been saving up for a holiday home on the Costa Blanca since they got married five years ago. With their first baby on the way, they want somewhere where they can soak up the Spanish sun on holidays and that they can share with family and friends. Scarlette Douglas tempts them with five properties in and around Torrevieja for their budget of £55,000

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien attempt to restore items including an old cheese press, and a 1980s motorbike almost gets the better of Henry and his restorer Guy

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Max McMurdo restore and upcycle 2CV headlamps, a children's rocking horse, a Victorian streetlamp and a high chair

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Angel goes shopping with Stephanie and shows her the best way to frame her father's original artwork using modern and antique fabric. Justin and Annabelle put up their newly renovated exterior chateau shutters, but the numbering seems to have gone a bit haywire, so getting the shutters on the right windows proves a challenge

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton helps Bill and Angie Jenkins, who plan to knock down their uneconomical bungalow on the bank of the River Thames and build a new home to fit in with the location. Potentially, the couple could have an investment profit of £330,000 by opting to self-build, and they visit a nearby property for inspiration on how to make their project more stylish

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Another selection of B&B owners take it in turns as hosts to decide which of their establishments provides the best value for money. The contest begins at Ian and Sam Sugden's Abbey House in Penrith, Cumbria, where the guests are impressed with the cleanliness of the rooms. However, there is some grumbling over breakfast about the bacon

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners stay at Compton House in Newark, Nottinghamshire, which is run by Lisa Holloway - who prides herself on providing a home away from home for her visitors. However, some of the guests have issues with dust and the haddock served at breakfast - and challenge whether a night's stay is worth the price tag

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Dave and Jo Bate welcome their guests to the White Horse, a pub and B&B in Ripon, North Yorkshire, where they hope an afternoon of morris dancing in front of the cathedral will make a lasting impression. Unfortunately, Ed and Jane Mitchell get locked inside their room by mistake, and there are arguments over tomatoes at breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Retired teachers Ed and Jane Mitchell welcome the contestants to the Old Farmhouse, Cheshire, but everyone is shocked to discover they will have to share a bathroom. Meanwhile, Sam and Ian Sugden find an unwelcome surprise underneath their bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Payment day arrives, as the guests gather to find out who has won and to thrash out their differences - from some fishy findings to a reminder of why sorry can be the hardest word. Later on, a heated exchange about cleaning ends in tears

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: George meets a young man who bought a 70-year-old boat for £5,000 that he intends to turn into a home, and to find a shipmate to help with the restoration, he turns to an internet dating site. There is also a cricket-mad grandad building a mini pavilion in his garden, with the 1950s-inspired build incorporating bats, bails and even a boundary rope alongside an ingenious bunk bed that with the pull of a chord transforms into a sofa. George's German road trip reaches Berlin, and a house situated where the Wall once stood

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Documentary revealing the hidden lives of the animals of Chester Zoo. The Asian elephant family welcome a new arrival. Grumpy matriarch Thi has been pregnant for almost two years and is reaching the end of her tether. She's given birth to a stillborn baby in the past, so the keepers have a close eye on her. Cameras also follow Red panda Jung, who has been getting the cold shoulder from his partner Nima since they had two cubs

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Amy and Marc create an eco-friendly sewage system by connecting the chateau's plumbing to two natural waste ponds 100 metres away. Ben and Billie go fossil hunting in their grounds

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Natasha Cargill wants to build a home shaped like two enormous periscopes in rural Norfolk, but to obtain planning permission, she has to ensure not only that the materials are sustainable, but also agree to measure the transportation used to deliver them. If these strict criteria are not met, she won't be allowed to live there, and to add to the pressure, she has just £330,000 to spend on construction and six months to complete the project. Kevin McCloud follows her progress

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Compilation episode of the most dramatic motorbike incidents the medics have dealt with, including a biker trapped with multiple injuries after crashing into a road sign

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Xintong is rushed to A&E after being flung from a go-kart at speed. Doctors are concerned that there may be damage to the 28 year old's spine and send her for urgent CT scans. Cameras also follow Gary, 58, who has come to St George's after trapping his finger between two shopping trolleys, causing it to swell enormously

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: This edition of the documentary features stories of human compassion and kindness in the most challenging circumstances. Allen, 82, has injured his chest after a dizzy spell caused him to fall down 13 steps. As son Graham waits nervously for doctors to assess Allen's injuries, Graham describes his dad's strength and resilience in carrying the family through dark times, and how precious the bond between father and son can be

programme.title: Food Unwrapped

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Angela Keenan and Jon Benbow-Brown have visited Menorca five times together, and have fallen in love with the laid-back way of life on the island. The pair are planning to retire there full-time, so Ben Hillman shows them five houses on the island for their budget of £165,000

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien explore a fairground, and collect a bicycle made for two, a ship's search lamp and a lambing chair

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In Gloucestershire, Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit landed gent Sir Benjamin Slade as they search for more upcycling prospects. Simon converts a gilded picture frame into a flat-screen TV surround, while Henry refurbishes a club fender fire surround and turns a quick profit on a Fordson tractor

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick visits Stephanie to check out the new boiler for her mum's apartment - but it's bad news and mum Isobel is not happy. Justin and Annabelle must get busy to complete rooms in time for a visit from Justin's 95-year-old grandmother. Jayne and Steve battle with a giant four poster bed for the master en suite bedroom, but with a little help from some friends, manage to complete another room

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton helps Andrew and Vicki Dodden from Cannington, Somerset, who plan to transform an 18th-century cottage into an eco-friendly home that is suitable for modern living. The couple stand to save £150,000 by opting to self-build, and visit a nearby house in Wells for inspiration on how they can tweak the design of their conversion to get the very best results

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Another selection of B&B owners take it in turns as hosts to decide which of their establishments provides the best value for money. They kick things off with an afternoon of laser mayhem, and continue the fun the next day by following breakfast with hands-on animal antics

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests head to the Arches on the Isle of Man for their second stay, where owner Phil Routledge hopes to wow them with scenic views and scuba diving at the B&B's own pool. However, some of the owners are dismayed by modern gadgets with a lack of instructions, and even find cobwebs in their rooms

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants arrive at the Trafford in Blackpool, owned by Robbie and John Welling, who are proud of their traditional 'bucket and spade' B&B. The guests are pleasantly surprised, even if previous host Phil is intent on finding dust as a bit of tit-for-tat retaliation, and the afternoon is spent learning the art of making sticks of rock

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Host Julie welcomes the contestants to Sheepy Lodge, Warwickshire, where they are shocked by their surroundings, and disappointed by a variety of things in the rooms. The host puts on some old-fashioned parlour games, before a fierce hide-and-seek contest erupts. At breakfast, Julie frets when she must prepare poached eggs, and is left emotional after the feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The rival B&B owners meet to settle some scores and discover what they have been paid. Knockaloe Beg Farm owners Fiona and John Anderson tackle criticisms of a missing host at breakfast, and Phil Routledge from The Arches on the Isle of Man takes on Robbie and John Welling from The Trafford in Blackpool about their 'forensic investigation'. After some heated discussion, the winner is announced

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: George meets a man planning to build Margate's first Victorian bathing machine in more than 100 years, and a coma survivor who proves he has a sense of humour by transforming a hearse into a family camper van. The presenter also visits a woman who has turned her narrowboat home into a floating bookshop, and in Germany he sees a lifeguard tower straight out of a 1950s sci-fi comic

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Chester's Penguin Island is home to one of the most enduring love stories in the zoo. At 22, Rud is the oldest on the island, and has been with his partner, Spike, for over 15 years. However, keepers are concerned that Rud's arthritic hip could be affecting his quality of life. Meanwhile, the Sumatran tigers are upgrading to a luxury new enclosure on the other side of the zoo as part of Chester's huge expansion. But they've lived in their home for most of their lives so it's not going to be an easy move

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Tim and Rebecca struggle to get their driveway ready for the arrival of their guests. Meanwhile, Amy and Marc unearth an original medieval fireplace

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows software executive Andy and garden designer Nicki, who are planning to build a 'floating' house on the Thames, which would be the first of its kind in the UK. Their architect has come up with an ambitious, experimental design that would see the whole property magically rise up with the flood waters. However, no one knows whether it will work, and the project soon becomes bogged down in the logistical challenges of bringing to fruition such a complex structure on a small island that is only accessible by a narrow footbridge

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer look back at multi-generation house searches, remembering how much more difficult it was when adding Grandma or Grandad into the mix

programme.title: New Amsterdam
programme.desc: Max is struggling to get back to normal three months after the accident and the birth of his daughter. Kapoor grapples with the stigma of ageing, Iggy has a successful day that inspires a life-changing idea and Reynolds deals with an unreliable new intern

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Twelve-year-old Brooke is rushed to St George's after suffering an asthma attack so severe that the paramedic team had to put her in an induced coma at the scene. Doctors are concerned that she may have dangerous amounts of fluid on her lungs and decide to take urgent chest X-rays. Howard, who's 76, arrives with burns to his face, hands and airways after trying to put out an explosion in his garden shed. As his worried wife waits for news in the relatives' room, doctors attempt to stabilise his condition and investigate the extent of his injuries

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Mrs Doyle wins a competition to meet a famous singer, and discreetly invites him round for tea - but the secret slips out, and soon the house is besieged by hordes of adoring pensioners. Comedy, starring Dermot Morgan, Ardal O'Hanlon and Pauline McLynn

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: A smooth-talking priest offers Ted a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to leave Craggy Island and run his own parish in Beverly Hills, but first he has to tell Jack, Dougal and Mrs Doyle that they are not invited. Final episode of the clerical comedy, starring Dermot Morgan, with Tommy Tiernan, Jeff Harding and a cameo appearance by music star Brian Eno

programme.title: New Amsterdam
programme.desc: Max is struggling to get back to normal three months after the accident and the birth of his daughter. Kapoor grapples with the stigma of ageing, Iggy has a successful day that inspires a life-changing idea and Reynolds deals with an unreliable new intern

Howard, who's 76, arrives with burns to his face, hands and airways after trying to put out an explosion in his garden shed. As his worried wife waits for news in the relatives' room, doctors attempt to stabilise his condition and investigate the extent of his injuries

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty heads to Thailand to find out about the surprising that makes a prawn cracker unique, while Matt Tebbutt visits Britain's largest producer of fresh herbs to learn about a new hi-tech method of extending shelf-life. Kate Quilton investigates what actually goes into sodium-replacement salts and examines whether salt really deserves its unhealthy reputation

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Steve Austin's Broken Skull Challenge
programme.desc: The former wrestler challenges eight contestants to triumph over a series of gruelling physical endurance tests

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: The guys go hog wild at a sprawling ranch in Texas, where Mike flips for a VW bus that has been transformed into a hunting lodge on wheels

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: Sean Kelly and the bidders are in Alton, Hampshire, where Linda is concerned that the other bidders are taking advantage of her superior knowledge and her looks. Meanwhile, Boudicca and Heavy D form more than just a business partnership, and Barry impresses with his dainty footwork

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: Sean Kelly and the bidders head to Poplar, East London, where Heavy D goes through his blue period after driving the other bidders up the wall with his antics

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: The guys uncover a former firefighter's stockpile, which includes an ultra-rare piece of Space Age history. Plus, Frank's plan goes up in smoke

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: The guys go big in Texas with a mega pick at the first Dr Pepper bottling plant, and tough negotiations in a 50,000 square-foot warehouse

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Documentary with unrestricted access to America's Customs and Border Protection working on the bridges that separate Texas and Mexico, following the work of the many officers who police the bridges and keep America safe. In the first episode, a shipment of cardboard packaging hides a deadly secret, and heroin disguised as a laptop baffles the world's most advanced narcotic detection device

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May are in Russia as the motoring magazine returns, taking part in a race across the urban landscape of St Petersburg. James defends the honour of the car in a very small Renault, Richard tries to prove that pedalling is faster by riding a bicycle, while Jeremy attempts to demonstrate that water is the quickest way across town by piloting a hovercraft. To make matters more complicated, all three presenters are racing not only one another but also the Stig, who is making his way toward the finish line by public transport. Plus, the new Lamborghini Huracan is put through its paces and singer-songwriter Ed Sheeran takes to the test track in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: The naturalist ventures into uncharted territory in pursuit of new discoveries. He takes on physical challenges, encounters extraordinary wildlife and meets remarkable people. In the first edition, Steve and a team of climbers and jungle survival experts head into the ancient landscape of South America's Guiana Shield. This vast wilderness of pristine forests and towering table-top mountains is one of the most inaccessible terrains on the planet

programme.title: Rick Stein's Spain
programme.desc: The chef continues his culinary tour of Spain by travelling east from the Basque region toward the Mediterranean coast, sampling a selection of great dishes and enjoying local hospitality along the way

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The presenters embark on a road trip across Australia's Northern Territory in fast, luxurious GT cars, with Jeremy Clarkson choosing a BMW M6 Gran Coupe, Richard Hammond picking a Bentley Continental GT V8 and James May opting for a Nissan GT-R. The trio set out on an adventure that takes in crocodile-infested rivers, a race around a vast opencast mine and an encounter with one of the world's longest lorries, concluding with an unusual challenge involving a farm, their cars and 4,000 cows. Plus, 24 star Kiefer Sutherland takes to the track in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: In the Jebel Akhdar mountain range of Oman, Steve leads an expedition into an unexplored desert canyon. With temperatures soaring, dehydration and heat stroke are a constant threat

programme.desc: Alice Levine, Alan Carr and Phill Jupitus join Alan Davies in answering questions from Sandi Toksvig on the subject of Quests

programme.desc: Rob Brydon hosts the comedy panel show, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by guests Dara O Briain, Denise Van Outen, Rhod Gilbert and Vernon Kay, who try to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

The comedian, actor, artist, DJ and musician treats the audience to his own peculiar brand of surreal humour, before introducing routines by two up-and-coming stars of the comedy circuit, with south Londoner Dane Baptiste and Irishman Al Porter taking to the stage at the famous venue

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Queen Elizabeth's Secret Agents
programme.desc: Documentary featuring dramatic reconstructions and interviews with historians to examine the network of spymasters and secret agents that helped protect Elizabeth I from assassination, terror and treason for more than 40 years. The first edition reveals how the pope's declaration of Elizabeth as a heretic 12 years into her reign set England's Catholics against her, leading the queen to charge William Cecil with the task of stopping assassins getting through, and the huge espionage network he established saw his spies break Catholic conspiracies at home and abroad

programme.title: Hitler's Propaganda Machine
programme.desc: The use of propaganda in Hitler's Nazi regime. The first episode examines the Fuehrer's fascination with propaganda, which began in the trenches of the First World War

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston travels to Portugal to meet leading experts on the Three Secrets of Fatima - a series of visions and prophecies allegedly given by the Virgin Mary to three young shepherds in 1917

programme.title: WW2 - Battles for Europe
programme.desc: Eye-witness veteran testimony from men who served under fire on the frontline, taking viewers to the heart of iconic moments in the history of warfare. The first episode looks at the Allied invasion of Europe, which began on June 6 1944 - D-Day. British, American and Canadian troops stormed the beaches of Normandy in what would become known as The Longest Day

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: Unexplained events during wartime, including German submarines that discovered previously unknown sea creatures and a Japanese bomb that floated on the jet stream

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off from Dereham in Norfolk on the final leg of his journey to the seaside town of Cromer. Along the way he gets the opportunity to drive a heritage diesel train, discovers why Norfolk black turkeys appeared on the Victorian Christmas menu, and samples classic Cromer crab

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets out to follow the route of the historic Irish mail service from Ledbury in Herefordshire to Holyhead on Anglesey. He begins by tasting the Victorian drink perry, meeting a pedigree Hereford bull and visiting the world's first iron-framed building in Shrewsbury, Shropshire

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team returns to Barrington Court in Somerset, where Fiona Bruce examines the house's collection of distinctive wood paneling. Among the unusual finds brought in to be valued are a revolver that may have been owned by Buffalo Bill, a collection of rare German chocolate moulds, a set of signal flags used in the Normandy Landings, a pair of Russian ceramic figures and an engraved rock crystal, as well as a portrait of a woman wearing it

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek dives into the lost Jowett marque, as a Bradford-built 1952 Javelin finds an unexpected home. Paul and Dave uncover a Ford Escort that's been untouched for 30 years

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A 1970s Bond Bug three-wheeler divides opinion among Mathewsons customers, while a retired engineer reluctantly auctions off his late father's treasured 60s Jaguar Mark 2

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Abandoned projects of the space age, including the HARP gun in Barbados and the mountainside Barcroft Observatory in California

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: Featuring a look at sightings of UFOs during the Second World War, a US plan to use bats against Japan's cities, and a German attempt to persuade British POWs to change sides

programme.title: WW2 - Battles for Europe
programme.desc: This edition examines the efforts of the British and Canadians who fought elite German divisions to capture the city of Caen, the key to breaking out of Normandy

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team returns to Barrington Court in Somerset, where Fiona Bruce examines the house's collection of distinctive wood paneling. Among the unusual finds brought in to be valued are a revolver that may have been owned by Buffalo Bill, a collection of rare German chocolate moulds, a set of signal flags used in the Normandy Landings, a pair of Russian ceramic figures and an engraved rock crystal, as well as a portrait of a woman wearing it

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey along the route of the historic Irish mail service from Ledbury to Holyhead, stopping off in Coalbrookdale, Shropshire, to visit the world's first iron bridge. He also explores Wrexham's Chirk Castle, and tries his hand at making traditional Cheshire cheese

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey along the Irish mail route beginning in Chester, where he takes a tour of Roman remains, and then visits a secret Second World War chemical plant at Rhydymwyn, Flintshire. After spending the night in Llandudno, he heads to the Conwy estuary for a spot of mussel fishing

programme.title: The Architecture The Railways Built
programme.desc: Tim Dunn explores the disused tube station Down Street in Mayfair, that played a crucial tactical role during the Second World War, and tours Rotterdam's new Centraal station with the man who designed it

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of a North Yorkshire-based, family-run historic car auction business, which uncovers some remarkably rare vehicles in the picturesque village of Thornton-le-Dale

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: The presenter examines the social significance of cars in the 20th century. He begins by travelling to Germany, Italy and Russia to examine how dictators kick-started the mobilization of the masses, finding tales of design brilliance, abject failure, war, fraud and double dealing. James also learns about how the British motor industry blew a gift-wrapped chance to rule the world and he gets his own back with a stunt that means bad news for one of the planet's most hated cars

programme.title: The Two Ronnies
programme.desc: Barker and Corbett present more classic comedy sketches, with a song from Tina Charles and another episode in the gripping period drama Hampton Wick

programme.title: The Two Ronnies
programme.desc: Vintage sketches from Messrs Barker and Corbett, including Fancy Dress Party, Smart Cooking with Lionel Smart, and classic serial Hampton Wick. With music from Tina Charles

programme.title: Hitler's Propaganda Machine
programme.desc: The use of propaganda in Hitler's Nazi regime. The first episode examines the Fuehrer's fascination with propaganda, which began in the trenches of the First World War

programme.title: Queen Elizabeth's Secret Agents
programme.desc: Documentary featuring dramatic reconstructions and interviews with historians to examine the network of spymasters and secret agents that helped protect Elizabeth I from assassination, terror and treason for more than 40 years. The first edition reveals how the pope's declaration of Elizabeth as a heretic 12 years into her reign set England's Catholics against her, leading the queen to charge William Cecil with the task of stopping assassins getting through, and the huge espionage network he established saw his spies break Catholic conspiracies at home and abroad

programme.desc: The docudrama focuses on the machinations of ambitious aristocrat the Earl of Essex as he tries to take over Robert Cecil's spy network in an attempt to gain control over the ageing Elizabeth I and hold the power to choose the next king of England

programme.title: Hitler's Propaganda Machine
programme.desc: The decade of propaganda, impassioned speeches, newspaper articles, posters and campaigning that all led to one moment - Hitler being made Chancellor of Germany in January 1933

programme.title: Forbidden History

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Ashton Court in Bristol, where they examine a piece of street art by Banksy in a local doorway. Expert Rupert Maas reveals the difficulty of valuing graffiti by an artist whose identity is a closely guarded secret. Other finds include a 19th-century automaton that can smoke a cigarette, a nautilus shell engraved with a picture of the city's suspension bridge, a globe clock that provides a surprise to its owner and a posset pot that may have origins closer to home than expected

programme.title: Queen Elizabeth's Secret Agents
programme.desc: Elizabeth is dead and James VI of Scotland travels south to take the throne at the invitation of Robert Cecil, but the new king has his own ideas and has little time for the late queen's spymaster. Soon Cecil's problems worsen when learns that his arch enemy John Gerard has escaped detention and made contact with a splinter cell in the Catholic underground with a plan to blow-up Parliament. Cecil must negotiate between warring factions to bring down what would become known as the Gunpowder Plot

programme.title: Hitler's Propaganda Machine
programme.desc: As Hitler's ambitions spread beyond Germany, the world drew toward another great war. This one would be fought on many battlefields, but also for the hearts and minds of the people

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston travels to Sardinia to meet the scientists and archaeologists who have evidence that suggests giants once existed. Researchers have discovered abnormally large human skeletons in networks of elaborate caves, mines and tombs in Sardinia, China, the United States, northern India and South America. However, if the race did exist, it remains unclear as to why it has seemingly been eradicated from history

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the third night of the competition in Boston, Lincolnshire, Adrian hopes that his spicy food and surprises will help him scoop the £1,000 prize. programme.desc: Polish-born carer Sylwia hosts the fourth party of the competition in Boston, Lincolnshire, and hopes her European menu will charm her guests. programme.title: Come Dine with Me

Famous faces tell their childhood stories, while social historians, toy designers and advertising experts discuss which toys didn't quite make the list, those that have stood the test of time and which ones were more than likely broken by Boxing Day

programme.title: Cast Away (2000)
programme.desc: A manager for an American courier service has to fend for himself when a plane crash leaves him stranded alone on a desert island. Initially panic-stricken, he overcomes his terror to survive for four years on his own before building a raft and setting off on an epic journey back home. Adventure drama, starring Tom Hanks, Helen Hunt, Nick Searcy, Lari White and Chris Noth

The programme also explains the scientific reasons behind the wildfire that destroyed one of the UK's most precious nature reserves, as well as a look back at record-breaking days of both heat and rain

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody
programme.desc: In the conclusion of this two-part documentary, Victoria Breeden is taken into custody, but claims she is not fit to be interviewed, while police search her home for evidence that proves she has attempted to hire someone to murder her husband. Detectives are determined not to release her, but DC Louisa Abbott fears she is dealing with the cleverest criminal she has ever encountered

programme.title: Jimmy's Farm
programme.desc: As 2020 comes to an end, this special edition returns to Jimmy Doherty's Suffolk farm and wildlife park where there is festive spirit in the air. As the winter jobs get ticked off the list, Jimmy looks back at the highlights from spring and autumn, from escaping meerkat Steve McQueen and Littlefoot the lamb, to frolicking tapirs and tiny piglets. Now, in the season of goodwill to all, Jimmy and the team prepare for a very special Christmas present for Fern, the orphaned roe deer

programme.title: Chitty Flies Again with David Walliams

programme.desc: Ray tricks Debra into letting him play golf - but finds it difficult to put a good round together as he contends with a guilty conscience. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton

programme.desc: Debra worries that Ray is happier at work than at home and persuades him to set up an office in the house, but things do not turn out as she planned

programme.desc: Debra decides to have a go at writing a children's book, but makes the mistake of asking Ray to help - little realising his competitive streak will jeopardise the entire project. Ray Romano stars

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to La Grange, a suburb of Chicago, to help the owners of Cafe 36, an upmarket bistro threatening to destroy the marriage of its owners due to the scale of their financial losses. The head chef's attitude to quality control is driving customers away, and it is up to Gordon Ramsay to turn the business around

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Folk dancer Gay and drama teacher Stephen are relocating to Finistère in the north west of France, where they love the rugged coastline and beautiful medieval villages. Laura Hamilton lines up five properties for their £100,000 budget, hoping at least one will be just what they are looking for

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Ian Snowley and Kay Marriot fell in love with the volcanic landscapes and coasts of Lanzarote, and are now determined to buy a house that they can use to explore the island further. Scarlette Douglas shows the couple five properties, hoping to maximise their budget of £150,000

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Guest-house owners in Spain hope their music event will not attract the local police. In France, a motorhome rental business deals with damaged vehicles and the owners of a holiday village in Portugal deal with an unfortunate leak

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer meet Nitasha and Nikunj in Staines-upon-Thames, who are unsure whether to sell their four-bed dormer bungalow or renovate instead. Having been reluctant to buy it just two and a half years ago, Nitasha has made the place home and believes it has the potential to be perfect for their future family. However, Nikunj feels like the work on the house will never be done, and would prefer to look for somewhere new

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: Make Do and Mend
programme.desc: Dick and Angel Strawbridge use the handiwork and design skills with which they have renovated their French chateau to give viewers ideas on fixing up their own homes. In the first show, Angel helps Miriam transform her 1970s bathroom into a relaxing sanctuary - without changing either the suite or the tiles - and Dick digs out his old handmade smoker to help teach Phil the ancient art of cheese smoking. The couple also advise Tom and Sim how to bring their unique front door into the 21st century

programme.title: The Man Who Fell from the Sky
programme.desc: In 2015, two men stowed away in the landing gear of a British Airways flight from Johannesburg to Heathrow. One of them fell to his death in Richmond - making worldwide headlines - but miraculously his companion survived an 11-hour journey with temperatures reaching -60 C. In this documentary, Richard Bentley sets out to uncover who they were, how they got on the plane, and why they decided to undertake such a dangerous and desperate mission

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows more of Britain's most ambitious self-building projects, as intrepid individuals attempt to design and construct the home of their dreams. In this first edition, Kevin meets former army captain Justin, who has spent £1.8million on an abandoned neo-Gothic cemetery keeper's lodge and adjoining ex-council toilet block in south-west London. Justin's vision is to restore the lodge but demolish the ugly toilet blocks and replace them with an enormous luxury ground-floor extension, with an added moat and a giant basement with a swimming pool - all just metres from the nearest grave

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: Architect George Clarke delves into the world of ingenious small builds, meeting people creating places to live, work and play in the most unlikely of places. Here he meets a couple who turned an old fire engine into a bar to raise money for firefighters' charities and a 23-year-old who built a shepherd's hut from scratch, including the nuts and bolts. George also visits an extraordinary home in Chile and Will Hardie builds a telescope

programme.title: How to Lose Weight Well
programme.desc: Doctors Helen Lawal and Javid Abdelmoneim help people try out the most popular diets on the market, with participants being put on a variety of regimens. Sisters Lesley and Debra from Essex road-test diets followed by celebrities Adele and Rebel Wilson, while friends Temi and Bose try out intermittent fasting, and drinking pals Maurice and Mick want to lose weight before attempting a Three Peaks walk in their native Yorkshire

programme.title: Food Unwrapped

programme.desc: Twenty-one-year-old medical student Sepii comes from a wealthy family, and has a huge collection of expensive designer shoes. But now she faces a reality check when she spends time with the Hussains, a family from Manchester who are living on the breadline

programme.title: World's Strongest Man
programme.desc: James Richardson presents action from the Giants Live Series event in Norway, which was the fourth qualifier for the World's Strongest Man heats. Several of Scandinavia's finest were involved, along with Brighton physiotherapist Lloyd Renals, with disciplines including the Snowmobile Drag and Viking Press

programme.title: World's Strongest Man
programme.desc: James Richardson presents action from the Giants Live Series event in Gateshead, which was the final qualifier for the World's Strongest Man heats. This also represented the Britain's Strongest Man event, with Laurence Shahlaei back to defend his title against the likes of Terry Hollands, with disciplines including the Shield Carry

programme.title: World's Strongest Man
programme.desc: James Richardson presents action from the opening heat from Haitang Bay in Sanya, China, where the top two of six competitors would progress to the final. Britain's Strongest Man Laurence Shahlaei was among those involved, along with American duo Robert Oberst and Nick Best, with disciplines including the Truck Pull and the Atlas Stones

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: The series investigating residential disputes continues, turning attention to feuds that turned violent. Among the stories highlighted are that of former paratrooper Rod who was attacked outside his own home by a knife-wielding neighbour, and 71-year-old Michael, a Suffolk villager whose fellow residents claim he has systematically harassed them, culminating in an assault in which he punched one of his neighbours repeatedly - an incident that was caught on camera. The programme also considers the devastating consequences when personal altercations end up escalating into neighbourhood wars, revealing how a conflict between the Webbs and the Browns in transformed their street in Devon into a war-zone

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: The observational documentary shedding light on residential disputes concludes with an insight into how suburban feuds can breed anger, suspicion and fear, and cause people to resort to subterfuge to get their way. Among the stories highlighted are that of Leslie, whose neighbours began capturing his activities on film, convinced that his trade business was illegal, and Phil, who was assigned a restraining order after his ex-mayor neighbour made recordings that proved he was harassing her. Also featured are two tales in which old friendships were ruined when certain parties resorted to breaching each other's privacy and round-the-clock surveillance

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents Stewart and Iain head to Manchester to recover one of the largest debts they have ever chased - more than £400,000 - owed to a builder after he was failed to receive payment for his work on a house. Meanwhile, officers Gary and Cona are in Kent to chase over £11,000 owed by a couple for unpaid rent

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: An evasive man in west London is given a choice between paying up or relinquishing his luxury car, there's a writ for the organiser of a music festival, an aggressive rent defaulter needs evicting, and an entire hole family is angered in Hertfordshire when the agents call to collect a loan owed to a woman's ex-partner

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents Max and Steve in East London try to recover over £7,000 owed by a restaurant owner for an unpaid gas bill, while officers Gareth and Mitch in Gloucestershire seek just under £3,000 from the owner of a car wash. Stewart and Vic are in Manchester trying to recover nearly £5,000 owed by a blinds and shutters supplier to a dissatisfied customer

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Gareth and Mitch are on the trail of £2,500 in unpaid parking tickets in Cardiff, and while the debtor is not at home, they notice his car is sporting false number plates and call the police. The duo also face an angry defendant in Swindon when trying to collect £2,000 for unpaid rent on a lock-up, while in Buckinghamshire, Matt and Garry consider seizing a haul of luxury watches to pay more than £16,000 owed to a landlord

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Yashvi apologises to Shane and he is glad to reconnect, but things with Dipi are not so simple. It is party season, and Roxy decides to loosen things up between Levi and Bea

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Mac tries to take Ari's mind off missing Tane by organising a night in. Colby and Taylor catch up in secret, unaware that Angelo is following their every move

programme.title: Fergie & Andrew: The Duke & Duchess of Disaster
programme.desc: Documentary examining the scandals that have surrounded Prince Andrew and Sarah Ferguson since their marriage in 1986 and subsequent divorce. The programme examines the succession of PR gaffes and undercover tabloid stings that followed in the Duchess of York's wake once she was no longer part of the royal family, as well as looking into the prince's friendship with convicted paedophile Jeffrey Epstein

programme.title: Meghan & Harry: The New Revelations
programme.desc: A look at the events that led to the Duke and Duchess of Sussex stepping back from the royal family, with the couple growing frustrated with a pecking order that left them overshadowed by others. This documentary delves into new claims and explores the speculation contained in recent books about the royals, testing their credibility and voracity and trying to unravel what actually happened

programme.title: Inside
programme.desc: How the budget hotel has become one of the UK's biggest chains, and interior designer Shaun Clarkson reveals the design tricks that help make rooms feel bigger than they are. programme.title: Adults Only
programme.desc: Cameras focus on Mia, who sells her trade from apartments across Birmingham. She owes £3,000 in rent and is facing possible eviction - unless she can earn enough money to keep a roof over her head

programme.title: Adults Only
programme.desc: People making a living from selling sex, including a couple who run their burgeoning porn film business from their house in Southend while also looking after their children, and a 70 -year-old dominatrix working from a dungeon in her semi-detached house in Swindon

programme.title: Sinkholes: Buried Underground
programme.desc: Geologist Tom Backhouse investigates sinkholes caused by collapsed mine shafts, including one in Gosforth that opened up in front of a flat and led down to an abandoned coal mine. In Bury St Edmunds, a homeowner reveals how he is stuck in an unsellable house that is surrounded by sinkholes

programme.title: World's Strongest Man
programme.desc: James Richardson presents action from the fourth heat from Haitang Bay in Sanya, China, where the top two of six competitors would progress to the final. Reigning champion Zydrunas Savickas was among those involved in this heat, along with England's Mark Felix, with disciplines including the Loading Race, Atlas Stones and Truck Pull

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: The De Ropers' hopes of a peaceful life in Yeovil, Somerset, were rudely shattered by their neighbour Peter Stoodley's 120 noisy chickens, and they spent their first two years in their new home battling to persuade the authorities to ban him from keeping animals. In a quiet Anglesey village, 67-year-old Pamela Booth's discovered that her neighbour had been fraudulently using her bank card to steal more than £1,000 over 10 months, while a 24-year-old wannabe rapper in Dagenham, east London, generated more than 80 complaints after repeatedly keeping residents awake by playing loud music into the early hours in his flat

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: In the Worcestershire village of Broadway, two families were taken advantage of by a cafe owner to whom they lent £120,000 between them, each unaware that they were not the sole investor. In Bristol, Tim Wrigley ended up being on the receiving end of intimidation and abuse after tiring of his neighbour's increasing demands and bizarre gifts, while a Nottingham mother and son resort to filming their noisy neighbour after months of endless loud music, shouting and personal threats

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents Max and Steve in East London try to recover over £7,000 owed by a restaurant owner for an unpaid gas bill, while officers Gareth and Mitch in Gloucestershire seek just under £3,000 from the owner of a car wash. Stewart and Vic are in Manchester trying to recover nearly £5,000 owed by a blinds and shutters supplier to a dissatisfied customer

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Stewart and Elmor set out to evict a tenant from a property in Manchester but when the man claims he knew nothing about the court order tempers begin to fray. A car salesman in Cumbria is not happy about paying £4,500 to a dissatisfied customer, and in Shropshire a pub licensee claims she is unable to pay the rent she owes to her ex-landlord. With emotions running high, can she cut a new deal?

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Stewart and Elmor are in Manchester to recover £4,000 owed to a firm of architects, only for the debtor to attempt to throw them off the scent. programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

The duo also chase £5,000 for an unpaid parking fine, which comes as a shock to the debtor, who has clearly hit hard times

programme.desc: A look at the extreme mobility problems caused by obesity. programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: A family who have generated more than 600 complaints from angry neighbours move into a quiet suburban street in Blackburn. But when her mother also gets involved, tempers start to fray. Stewart and Iain confront a woman in Cheshire who is refusing to pay fees to a veterinary practice, claiming her dog has been misdiagnosed, while Paul and Max collect £5,000 owed by a banned company director in east London accused of mis-selling assets on the stock market

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.desc: This edition of the programme focuses on large families who depend on the benefits system, including 64-year-old Peter Rolfe, whose children - by 15 different women - range in ages from three to 43, of which he is the sole carer for six. He has been fighting his council in the Isle of Wight for a much bigger house and is also at loggerheads with the benefits office for capping his weekly payment. West Midlands mother-of-eight Marie Buchanan has recently split from her partner and is looking for a larger home, while Ipswich couple the Fisks have been in dispute with their neighbours, who objected to two houses being knocked into one to accommodate them and their 13 children

programme.title: The Island (2005)

Sci-fi thriller, starring Ewan McGregor, Scarlett Johansson, Sean Bean and Steve Buscemi

programme.desc: A sniper begins a campaign of apparent random shootings in the city. The team knows that the killings are connected and identifies the criminal as a missing man who was subjected to abuse as a child, but Chief Carver refuses to make the information public, clashing with Mac in the process. Crime drama, starring Gary Sinise and John Larroquette

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Part two of two. Mac continues the investigation into the killing of Carver's sister and discovers a bullet lodged in the corpse's chest that matches a gun registered to the chief. However, Jo warns him of the consequences of accusing a senior officer of murder. Starring Gary Sinise and John Larroquette

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Jo discover the body of a department store manager in a shop window when they apprehend a pickpocket. They suspect the thief of murder, but his excuse leads them to the victim's assistant, who appears to have planned the crime. However, they unearth evidence that her dead boss had been accused of sexual harassment and had wounds on his body indicating that he might also have been an assailant. Drama, starring Gary Sinise and Sela Ward

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A woman's body is found in a lift, and McCoy finds his prosecution of the prime suspect hampered by a vindictive judge Ross offended in court

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A pregnant security guard is injured when a package explodes at a scientific research lab, prompting Green and Lupo to investigate a chemist's latest experiments. It transpires that research is being undertaken to establish whether it is possible to isolate the 'gay gene' in unborn foetuses. Jesse L Martin and Jeremy Sisto star

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A dispute over a pair of trousers missing from a dry cleaner's ends in murder - and the ensuing investigation leads Lupo and Green to a businessman working for a shady multinational chain of department stores. Drama, starring Jesse L Martin and Jeremy Sisto

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A spy satellite technician's voyeuristic activities inadvertently lead to him witnessing the murder of a Navy commander involved in an important weapons project. Tipped off by an anonymous call, the investigators arrive at the base to find the victim has been stabbed in the back. Drama, starring Mark Harmon and Sasha Alexander

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A severed leg bearing a marine tattoo is discovered in a rubbish bin. Once the victim is identified, Gibbs and the crew follow the trail back to a small town, where an insurance fraud seems to have taken place. American naval crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum and Sasha Alexander

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team races to find a sniper who is targeting marine recruitment officers and picking them off one by one. The only clue is a white feather left at the scene of each shooting - and in the absence of any other evidence, Gibbs uses himself as bait to catch the killer. American drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Sasha Alexander and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Three similar execution-style killings take place, and the team discovers one of the victims was going through a messy divorce. His widow falls under suspicion of committing the first two crimes to mask the murder of her husband - but, unfortunately for the agents, she has an alibi. Starring Michael Weatherly and David McCallum

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: Part two of two. Goren comes under intense pressure when he battles his old enemy Elizabeth Hitchens, who embarks on a bloody campaign to discredit the detective and break his spirit. The struggle with his bitterest enemy will threaten his very survival. Olivia D'Abo guest stars, with Vincent D'Onofrio

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The detectives arrest two students suspected of raping and murdering a cancer patient who grew marijuana in her apartment. The pair blame each other, so the prosecution team is faced with the dilemma of charging the older boy as an adult, despite evidence that he was not mentally capable of carrying out the crimes. Christopher Meloni stars

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A 14-year-old rape victim tries to commit suicide by jumping off an underground platform. As Benson and Stabler search for her attacker, DNA tests unearth a dark family secret involving the two main suspects and the victim's parents. Drama, starring Mariska Hargitay and Christopher Meloni

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Cabot takes a child abuse case, determined to find evidence that will convict the defendant. Her personal feelings tempt her to bend the rules, leading Stabler and Benson into an illegal search of the victim's home - a move that threatens all of their careers

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Detectives Benson and Stabler check the handbag of a suspected rape victim for identification and discover the severed finger of a small child in a plastic bag. A murder investigation is launched and Tutuola uncovers a series of traumatic events experienced by the woman in question during childhood

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Adam witnesses a murder on a video chat website, and the team races to the victim's apartment, where a search reveals a significant clue - her laptop has gone missing. Inquiries eventually lead to a college professor who implicates the dead girl's boyfriend - and as the case unfolds, it emerges that the killer is lining up a second victim. Crime drama, starring AJ Buckley, Gary Sinise and Sela Ward

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Armed robbers break into the apartment of lawyer Roland Carson and make off in a helicopter with jewels worth more than $5million. The detectives investigate and, as people who have come into contact with the gems start to turn up dead, they suspect Carson could have staged the whole affair and killed his accomplices to cover his tracks. Drama, starring Gary Sinise and Carmine Giovinazzo

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces around the nation to discover how CCTV and technological advances are helping to bring criminals to justice. The programme reveals how essential surveillance cameras have become in the fight against crime and features footage of people caught red-handed

Guest starring singer Ne-Yo

Mac recognises the victim as conspiracy-obsessed Tessa James, and as he works to explore whether there was truth in her stories, the investigation leads to an elite gentlemen's club that has traces of blood on the walls. Starring Gary Sinise and Sela Ward

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A student is found stabbed to death in his flat, and a college friend is soon named prime suspect - but she claims the victim was a stranger who used a date rape drug on her. However, it soon emerges that the crime could have been racially motivated. Drama, with Jerry Orbach and Benjamin Bratt

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Green are called to the scene after a white teenage boy and a black girl are killed in a shooting near a neighbourhood playground. However, progress is slow when the detectives find locals unwilling to talk about the events, but two brothers who were friends of the murdered boy point them towards a suspect. Jeremy Sisto and Jesse L Martin star

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A politician is embroiled in a triple murder, but persuading him to reveal the truth is only the first step toward unmasking a killer willing to go to any lengths to avoid capture. Crime drama, starring Sam Waterston and Jesse L Martin

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team races to find a sniper who is targeting marine recruitment officers and picking them off one by one. The only clue is a white feather left at the scene of each shooting - and in the absence of any other evidence, Gibbs uses himself as bait to catch the killer. American drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Sasha Alexander and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS

Starring Michael Weatherly and David McCallum

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs entrusts the team with the task of finding his former commanding officer, who has gone on the run rather than staying behind to face charges of killing a marine and stealing funds. Kate and Tony's investigation leads them to the missing man's cabin - which has been sprayed with bullets and contains an unidentified corpse. Mark Harmon stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Ducky, Gerald and Kate are taken hostage by a gunman who demands evidence relating to the recent shooting of a suspected terrorist. As Gibbs and the team struggle to resolve the crisis, they are alarmed to discover there is a Hamas operative in their midst. Crime drama, guest starring Alan Dale (Ugly Betty)

programme.title: The Murders
programme.desc: Rookie cop Kate Jameson is partnered with Detective Mike Huntley as they investigate a serial killer who uses music in destructive ways. Investigative drama, starring Jessica Lucas

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A six-year-old girl tests positive for a sexually transmitted disease - and the team turns to her family to trace the paedophile who abused her. However, revelations about the victim's elder sister place Cabot in a tricky situation. Starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Stephanie March

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: An infant ingests cocaine that has been mixed into baby food but Internal Affairs blocks the investigation, so the team goes undercover to expose a drug ring run by corrupt cops. Stabler is consigned to desk duty after a major operation takes a turn for the worse and he is forced to shoot a suspect in self-defence. However, Cragen and Benson uncover a smuggling racket as they pursue the case

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Benson and Stabler investigate an assault on a restaurateur's wife, and find the attack is similar to a rape committed the previous week. They discover a locksmith visited both women's houses before the incident, but when they examine the stolen goods found in his toolbox, a new connection emerges - putting another suspect in the frame. Crime drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The murder of a transvestite in a church throws suspicion on its resident priest, who Benson and Stabler discover has a history of sexual abuse charges. They try to talk to the cleric's previous victims, who are reluctant to get involved because of a confidentiality agreement they signed - but when one of them finally opens up, new light is thrown on the culprit's identity

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: A couple whose student son has been missing for a week fear the worst when they discover a badly decomposed body on the roof of a downtown warehouse, and the CSIs are called to investigate further. However, the case is turned on its head when the autopsy reveals the dead man's true identity, and an anonymous phone call leads the team on a wild chase through the city. Crime drama, guest starring Helen Slater

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: A sniper begins a campaign of apparent random shootings in the city. The team knows that the killings are connected and identifies the criminal as a missing man who was subjected to abuse as a child, but Chief Carver refuses to make the information public, clashing with Mac in the process. Crime drama, starring Gary Sinise and John Larroquette

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces around the nation to discover how CCTV and technological advances are helping to bring criminals to justice. The programme reveals how essential surveillance cameras have become in the fight against crime and features footage of people caught red-handed
